For anihpj services, monitoring PDB status is critical for understanding service resilience during deployments and maintenance. The disruptionsAllowed field indicates how many pods can be safely terminated without violating the budget, helping operations teams plan maintenance windows. Conditions provide detailed information about budget health, including sync failures or insufficient pod scenarios.

Syntax
# Get PodDisruptionBudget status
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget pdb-name -n namespace -o jsonpath='{.status}'

# Check disruptions allowed
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget pdb-name -n namespace -o jsonpath='{.status.disruptionsAllowed}'

# View current healthy pods
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget pdb-name -n namespace -o jsonpath='{.status.currentHealthy}'
Parameters Description
  • currentHealthy (int32, required): Current number of healthy pods matching the selector
  • desiredHealthy (int32, required): Minimum desired number of healthy pods based on spec constraints
  • disruptionsAllowed (int32, required): Number of pod disruptions currently allowed without violating the budget
  • expectedPods (int32, required): Total number of pods counted by this disruption budget
  • conditions ([]Condition): Conditions for PDB status
    • DisruptionAllowed: Indicates if disruptions are currently permitted
    • SyncFailed: Controller encountered error computing allowed disruptions
    • InsufficientPods: Pod count at or below required number
    • SufficientPods: More pods than required exist
  • disruptedPods (map[string]Time): Pods whose eviction was processed but not yet observed by controller
  • observedGeneration (int64): Most recent generation observed when updating status
Return Value

Returns a PodDisruptionBudgetStatus object containing current health metrics, allowed disruptions, conditions, and disrupted pods map.

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Skills Service Status Overview

View Skills PDB status showing current health and allowed disruptions.

# Get Skills PDB status
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget skills-api-pdb -n skills-service -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq
{ "currentHealthy": 4, "desiredHealthy": 2, "disruptionsAllowed": 2, "expectedPods": 4, "observedGeneration": 1 }
Example 2: User Service Disruptions Allowed

Check how many User service pods can be disrupted currently.

# Check User PDB disruptions allowed
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget user-api-pdb -n user-service -o jsonpath='{.status.disruptionsAllowed}'
1
Example 3: Author Service PDB with Conditions

Display Author PDB status including condition information.

# Describe Author PDB for full status
kubectl describe poddisruptionbudget author-api-pdb -n author-service
Name: author-api-pdb Namespace: author-service Min available: 80% Selector: app=author-api,tier=backend Status: Allowed disruptions: 1 Current: 5 Desired: 4 Total: 5 Events:
Example 4: Location Service Health Metrics

Extract Location PDB current vs desired healthy pod counts.

# Get Location PDB health metrics
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget location-api-pdb -n location-service -o json | jq '.status | {currentHealthy, desiredHealthy, expectedPods}'
{ "currentHealthy": 3, "desiredHealthy": 2, "expectedPods": 3 }
Example 5: JobPost Service Complete Status

View comprehensive JobPost PDB status with all fields.

# Get JobPost PDB full status
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget jobpost-api-pdb -n jobpost-service -o yaml | grep -A 20 status:
status: conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-12-15T10:30:00Z" message: "" observedGeneration: 1 reason: SufficientPods status: "True" type: DisruptionAllowed currentHealthy: 5 desiredHealthy: 3 disruptionsAllowed: 2 expectedPods: 5 observedGeneration: 1

PodDisruptionBudgetList

Full Description

PodDisruptionBudgetList is a collection of PodDisruptionBudgets. It provides a way to list and manage multiple PDBs across namespaces or within a specific namespace. The list includes all PodDisruptionBudget objects matching the query criteria, along with their current status and configuration.

For the anihpj application, listing PDBs across all service namespaces helps ensure comprehensive high-availability configuration. Operations teams can audit all disruption budgets for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost services to verify that critical APIs have appropriate protection during maintenance windows and deployment operations.

Syntax
# List PodDisruptionBudgets in specific namespace
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets -n namespace

# List all PodDisruptionBudgets across all namespaces
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets --all-namespaces

# List PDBs with custom columns
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets --all-namespaces -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,MIN-AVAILABLE:.spec.minAvailable
Parameters Description
  • apiVersion: policy/v1 - API version
  • kind: PodDisruptionBudgetList - resource type
  • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata including resourceVersion and continue token for pagination
  • items ([]PodDisruptionBudget, required): Array of PodDisruptionBudget objects
Return Value

Returns a PodDisruptionBudgetList object containing an array of PodDisruptionBudget items and list metadata.

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List Skills Service PDBs

Get all PodDisruptionBudgets in Skills namespace.

# List Skills namespace PDBs
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets -n skills-service
NAME MIN AVAILABLE MAX UNAVAILABLE ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS AGE skills-api-pdb 2 N/A 2 5d
Example 2: List User Service PDBs with Labels

Filter User service PDBs by app label.

# List User PDBs with label selector
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets -n user-service -l app=anihpj
NAME MIN AVAILABLE MAX UNAVAILABLE ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS AGE user-api-pdb N/A 1 1 5d
Example 3: List All anihpj PDBs

View all PodDisruptionBudgets across anihpj service namespaces.

# List all anihpj PDBs
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj
NAMESPACE NAME MIN AVAILABLE MAX UNAVAILABLE ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS AGE user-service user-api-pdb N/A 1 1 5d jobpost-service jobpost-api-pdb 3 N/A 2 5d
Example 4: List PDBs in JSON Format

Get Location service PDBs as JSON for programmatic processing.

# List Location PDBs as JSON
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets -n location-service -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, minAvailable: .spec.minAvailable, maxUnavailable: .spec.maxUnavailable}'
{ "name": "location-api-pdb", "minAvailable": null, "maxUnavailable": "50%" }
Example 5: Count Total PDBs

Count how many PodDisruptionBudgets exist across anihpj cluster.

# Count all PDBs
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets --all-namespaces --no-headers | wc -l
5

Operations

The following operations are available for managing PodDisruptionBudget resources in the anihpj jobpost application.

get - Read the specified PodDisruptionBudget
get (status) - Read status of PodDisruptionBudget
list - List PDBs in namespace
list (all namespaces) - List PDBs cluster-wide
create - Create a PodDisruptionBudget
update - Replace PodDisruptionBudget
update (status) - Replace PDB status
patch - Partially update PDB
patch (status) - Partially update PDB status
delete - Delete a PodDisruptionBudget
deletecollection - Delete multiple PDBs

get - read the specified PodDisruptionBudget

Retrieve detailed information about a specific PodDisruptionBudget in a namespace.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/poddisruptionbudgets/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PodDisruptionBudget
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace containing the PDB
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PodDisruptionBudget): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Skills Service PDB

Retrieve Skills service PodDisruptionBudget in YAML format.

# Get Skills PDB
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget skills-api-pdb -n skills-service -o yaml
apiVersion: policy/v1 kind: PodDisruptionBudget metadata: name: skills-api-pdb namespace: skills-service spec: minAvailable: 2 selector: matchLabels: app: skills-api status: currentHealthy: 4 desiredHealthy: 2 disruptionsAllowed: 2 expectedPods: 4
Example 2: Get User Service PDB with Describe

View detailed User service PDB information including status.

# Describe User PDB
kubectl describe poddisruptionbudget user-api-pdb -n user-service
Name: user-api-pdb Namespace: user-service Labels: app=anihpj service=user Spec: Max unavailable: 1 Selector: app=user-api Unhealthy Pod Eviction Policy: IfHealthyBudget Status: Allowed disruptions: 1 Current: 3 Desired: 2 Total: 3 Events:
Example 3: Get Author Service PDB in JSON

Retrieve Author service PDB as JSON.

# Get Author PDB in JSON
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget author-api-pdb -n author-service -o json
{ "apiVersion": "policy/v1", "kind": "PodDisruptionBudget", "metadata": { "name": "author-api-pdb", "namespace": "author-service" }, "spec": { "minAvailable": "80%", "selector": { "matchLabels": { "app": "author-api", "tier": "backend" } } } }
Example 4: Get Location Service PDB Min Available

Extract specific field from Location service PDB.

# Get Location PDB maxUnavailable
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget location-api-pdb -n location-service -o jsonpath='{.spec.maxUnavailable}'
50%
Example 5: Get JobPost Service PDB Complete Info

View comprehensive JobPost service PDB information.

# Get JobPost PDB
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget jobpost-api-pdb -n jobpost-service -o yaml
apiVersion: policy/v1 kind: PodDisruptionBudget metadata: labels: app: anihpj component: api tier: backend name: jobpost-api-pdb namespace: jobpost-service spec: minAvailable: 3 selector: matchLabels: app: jobpost-api environment: production unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy: IfHealthyBudget status: currentHealthy: 5 desiredHealthy: 3 disruptionsAllowed: 2 expectedPods: 5

get - read status of the specified PodDisruptionBudget

Retrieve status information for a specific PodDisruptionBudget.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/poddisruptionbudgets/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PodDisruptionBudget
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace containing the PDB
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PodDisruptionBudget): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Skills Service PDB Status

Check Skills service PDB current health status.

# Get Skills PDB status
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget skills-api-pdb -n skills-service -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq
{ "currentHealthy": 4, "desiredHealthy": 2, "disruptionsAllowed": 2, "expectedPods": 4, "observedGeneration": 1 }
Example 2: Check User Service Disruptions Allowed

View how many User service pods can be disrupted.

# Check User PDB disruptions allowed
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget user-api-pdb -n user-service -o jsonpath='{.status.disruptionsAllowed}'
1
Example 3: Get Author Service PDB Health Metrics

Extract Author service PDB health metrics from status.

# Get Author PDB health metrics
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget author-api-pdb -n author-service -o json | jq '.status | {currentHealthy, desiredHealthy}'
{ "currentHealthy": 5, "desiredHealthy": 4 }
Example 4: Monitor Location Service PDB Status

View Location service PDB status with conditions.

# Describe Location PDB status
kubectl describe poddisruptionbudget location-api-pdb -n location-service | grep -A 10 Status:
Status: Allowed disruptions: 1 Current: 3 Desired: 2 Total: 3
Example 5: Get JobPost Service Complete Status

Retrieve full JobPost service PDB status with conditions.

# Get JobPost PDB complete status
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget jobpost-api-pdb -n jobpost-service -o yaml | grep -A 15 status:
status: conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-12-15T10:30:00Z" message: "" observedGeneration: 1 reason: SufficientPods status: "True" type: DisruptionAllowed currentHealthy: 5 desiredHealthy: 3 disruptionsAllowed: 2 expectedPods: 5 observedGeneration: 1

list - list or watch objects of kind PodDisruptionBudget

List PodDisruptionBudgets in a specific namespace with optional filtering.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/poddisruptionbudgets
Parameters
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace to list PDBs from
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - allow watch bookmarks
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter results
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter results
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (PodDisruptionBudgetList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List Skills Service PDBs

Get all PodDisruptionBudgets in Skills namespace.

# List Skills PDBs
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets -n skills-service
NAME MIN AVAILABLE MAX UNAVAILABLE ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS AGE skills-api-pdb 2 N/A 2 5d
Example 2: List User Service PDBs with Labels

Filter User service PDBs by label selector.

# List User PDBs with label
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets -n user-service -l app=anihpj
NAME MIN AVAILABLE MAX UNAVAILABLE ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS AGE user-api-pdb N/A 1 1 5d
Example 3: List Author Service PDBs in Wide Format

View Author service PDBs with additional details.

# List Author PDBs wide output
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets -n author-service -o wide
NAME MIN AVAILABLE MAX UNAVAILABLE ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS AGE author-api-pdb 80% N/A 1 5d
Example 4: Watch Location Service PDBs

Monitor changes to Location service PDBs in real-time.

# Watch Location PDBs
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets -n location-service --watch
NAME MIN AVAILABLE MAX UNAVAILABLE ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS AGE location-api-pdb N/A 50% 1 5d
Example 5: List JobPost Service PDBs with Custom Columns

Display JobPost PDBs with specific column format.

# List JobPost PDBs with custom columns
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets -n jobpost-service -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,MIN-AVAILABLE:.spec.minAvailable,CURRENT:.status.currentHealthy
NAME MIN-AVAILABLE CURRENT jobpost-api-pdb 3 5

list - list or watch objects of kind PodDisruptionBudget (all namespaces)

List PodDisruptionBudgets across all namespaces in the cluster.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/policy/v1/poddisruptionbudgets
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - allow watch bookmarks
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter results
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter results
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (PodDisruptionBudgetList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj PDBs

View all PodDisruptionBudgets across anihpj namespaces.

# List all anihpj PDBs
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj
NAMESPACE NAME MIN AVAILABLE MAX UNAVAILABLE ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS AGE user-service user-api-pdb N/A 1 1 5d jobpost-service jobpost-api-pdb 3 N/A 2 5d
Example 2: List PDBs for All Services

Get PDBs across all Django service namespaces.

# List all PDBs
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets --all-namespaces
NAMESPACE NAME MIN AVAILABLE MAX UNAVAILABLE ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS AGE skills-service skills-api-pdb 2 N/A 2 5d user-service user-api-pdb N/A 1 1 5d author-service author-api-pdb 80% N/A 1 5d location-service location-api-pdb N/A 50% 1 5d jobpost-service jobpost-api-pdb 3 N/A 2 5d
Example 3: List PDBs with Custom Columns

Display cluster-wide PDBs with specific information.

# List all PDBs with custom columns
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets --all-namespaces -o custom-columns=NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,MIN:.spec.minAvailable,MAX:.spec.maxUnavailable
NAMESPACE NAME MIN MAX skills-service skills-api-pdb 2 user-service user-api-pdb 1 author-service author-api-pdb 80% location-service location-api-pdb 50% jobpost-service jobpost-api-pdb 3
Example 4: List PDBs in JSON Format

Get all PDBs as JSON for programmatic processing.

# List all PDBs as JSON
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | {namespace: .metadata.namespace, name: .metadata.name, disruptionsAllowed: .status.disruptionsAllowed}'
{ "namespace": "skills-service", "name": "skills-api-pdb", "disruptionsAllowed": 2 } { "namespace": "user-service", "name": "user-api-pdb", "disruptionsAllowed": 1 }
Example 5: Count Total PDBs

Count how many PodDisruptionBudgets exist in anihpj cluster.

# Count all PDBs
kubectl get poddisruptionbudgets --all-namespaces --no-headers | wc -l
5

create - create a PodDisruptionBudget

Create a new PodDisruptionBudget in a namespace to protect pods from voluntary disruptions.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/poddisruptionbudgets
Parameters
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace to create the PDB in
  • body: PodDisruptionBudget, required - PodDisruptionBudget object to create
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager creating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PodDisruptionBudget): OK
  • 201 (PodDisruptionBudget): Created
  • 202 (PodDisruptionBudget): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Create Skills Service PDB

Create PodDisruptionBudget for Skills API ensuring 2 pods minimum.

# Create Skills PDB from YAML
kubectl apply -f skills-api-pdb.yaml
poddisruptionbudget.policy/skills-api-pdb created
Example 2: Create User Service PDB

Set up User service PDB with maxUnavailable constraint.

# Create User PDB
kubectl create -f user-api-pdb.yaml
poddisruptionbudget.policy/user-api-pdb created
Example 3: Create Author Service PDB with Dry Run

Test Author service PDB creation without applying.

# Create Author PDB with dry-run
kubectl apply -f author-api-pdb.yaml --dry-run=server
poddisruptionbudget.policy/author-api-pdb created (server dry run)
Example 4: Create Location Service PDB with Percentage

Create Location service PDB with percentage-based constraint.

# Apply Location PDB
kubectl apply -f location-api-pdb.yaml
poddisruptionbudget.policy/location-api-pdb created
Example 5: Create Comprehensive JobPost PDB

Create complete JobPost service PDB with all options.

# Create JobPost PDB
kubectl apply -f jobpost-api-pdb.yaml
poddisruptionbudget.policy/jobpost-api-pdb created

update - replace the specified PodDisruptionBudget

Replace an existing PodDisruptionBudget with a new definition.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/poddisruptionbudgets/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PodDisruptionBudget
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace containing the PDB
  • body: PodDisruptionBudget, required - updated PodDisruptionBudget object
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager updating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PodDisruptionBudget): OK
  • 201 (PodDisruptionBudget): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Update Skills Service PDB Constraint

Update Skills PDB to change minAvailable from 2 to 3.

# Edit and update Skills PDB
kubectl edit poddisruptionbudget skills-api-pdb -n skills-service
poddisruptionbudget.policy/skills-api-pdb edited
Example 2: Update User Service PDB Strategy

Change User service PDB from maxUnavailable to minAvailable.

# Get, modify, and apply User PDB
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget user-api-pdb -n user-service -o yaml > user-pdb.yaml
# Edit user-pdb.yaml
kubectl apply -f user-pdb.yaml
poddisruptionbudget.policy/user-api-pdb configured
Example 3: Update Author Service PDB Selector

Update Author PDB selector to match additional labels.

# Replace Author PDB with kubectl replace
kubectl replace -f author-api-pdb-updated.yaml
poddisruptionbudget.policy/author-api-pdb replaced
Example 4: Update Location Service PDB Policy

Modify Location service unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.

# Update Location PDB with dry-run
kubectl apply -f location-api-pdb-v2.yaml --dry-run=server
poddisruptionbudget.policy/location-api-pdb configured (server dry run)
Example 5: Update JobPost Service Complete PDB

Update comprehensive JobPost PDB with new constraints.

# Apply updated JobPost PDB
kubectl apply -f jobpost-api-pdb-v2.yaml
poddisruptionbudget.policy/jobpost-api-pdb configured

update - replace status of the specified PodDisruptionBudget

Replace the status of an existing PodDisruptionBudget (typically used by controllers).

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/poddisruptionbudgets/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PodDisruptionBudget
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace containing the PDB
  • body: PodDisruptionBudget, required - PodDisruptionBudget object with updated status
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager updating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PodDisruptionBudget): OK
  • 201 (PodDisruptionBudget): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: View Skills Service PDB Status Endpoint

Note: Status updates are typically performed by controllers, not manually.

# Check Skills PDB status subresource
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget skills-api-pdb -n skills-service --subresource=status -o yaml
apiVersion: policy/v1 kind: PodDisruptionBudget metadata: name: skills-api-pdb namespace: skills-service status: currentHealthy: 4 desiredHealthy: 2 disruptionsAllowed: 2 expectedPods: 4
Example 2: Monitor User Service PDB Status Changes

Watch User PDB for status changes managed by controller.

# Watch User PDB status
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget user-api-pdb -n user-service --watch -o jsonpath='{.status.disruptionsAllowed}'
1
Example 3: Verify Author Service PDB Status Update

Confirm Author PDB status reflects pod changes.

# Check Author PDB status after scaling
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget author-api-pdb -n author-service -o jsonpath='{.status}'
{"currentHealthy":5,"desiredHealthy":4,"disruptionsAllowed":1,"expectedPods":5,"observedGeneration":1}
Example 4: Check Location Service PDB Status Conditions

View Location PDB status conditions set by controller.

# Get Location PDB conditions
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget location-api-pdb -n location-service -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions}'
[{"lastTransitionTime":"2025-12-15T10:30:00Z","message":"","observedGeneration":1,"reason":"SufficientPods","status":"True","type":"DisruptionAllowed"}]
Example 5: Monitor JobPost Service PDB Status

Track JobPost PDB status metrics over time.

# Describe JobPost PDB to see status
kubectl describe poddisruptionbudget jobpost-api-pdb -n jobpost-service | grep -A 10 Status:
Status: Conditions: Last Transition Time: 2025-12-15T10:30:00Z Message: Observed Generation: 1 Reason: SufficientPods Status: True Type: DisruptionAllowed Current Healthy: 5 Desired Healthy: 3 Disruptions Allowed: 2 Expected Pods: 5

patch - partially update the specified PodDisruptionBudget

Partially update a PodDisruptionBudget using strategic merge patch, JSON merge patch, or JSON patch.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/poddisruptionbudgets/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PodDisruptionBudget
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace containing the PDB
  • body: Patch, required - patch to apply
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager patching this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • force (in query): boolean - force apply the patch
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PodDisruptionBudget): OK
  • 201 (PodDisruptionBudget): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Patch Skills Service PDB minAvailable

Update Skills PDB minAvailable using JSON patch.

# Patch Skills PDB to change minAvailable
kubectl patch poddisruptionbudget skills-api-pdb -n skills-service --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/minAvailable", "value": 3}]'
poddisruptionbudget.policy/skills-api-pdb patched
Example 2: Patch User Service PDB Labels

Update labels on User PDB using merge patch.

# Patch User PDB labels
kubectl patch poddisruptionbudget user-api-pdb -n user-service --type=merge -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"environment":"production"}}}'
poddisruptionbudget.policy/user-api-pdb patched
Example 3: Patch Author Service PDB Selector

Add label to Author PDB selector.

# Patch Author PDB selector
kubectl patch poddisruptionbudget author-api-pdb -n author-service --type=merge -p='{"spec":{"selector":{"matchLabels":{"version":"v2"}}}}'
poddisruptionbudget.policy/author-api-pdb patched
Example 4: Patch Location Service PDB Eviction Policy

Change Location PDB unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.

# Patch Location PDB eviction policy
kubectl patch poddisruptionbudget location-api-pdb -n location-service --type=merge -p='{"spec":{"unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy":"IfHealthyBudget"}}'
poddisruptionbudget.policy/location-api-pdb patched
Example 5: Patch JobPost Service PDB Annotations

Add annotations to JobPost PDB.

# Patch JobPost PDB annotations
kubectl patch poddisruptionbudget jobpost-api-pdb -n jobpost-service --type=merge -p='{"metadata":{"annotations":{"description":"Production JobPost API disruption budget"}}}'
poddisruptionbudget.policy/jobpost-api-pdb patched

patch - partially update status of the specified PodDisruptionBudget

Partially update the status of a PodDisruptionBudget (typically used by controllers).

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/poddisruptionbudgets/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PodDisruptionBudget
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace containing the PDB
  • body: Patch, required - patch to apply
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager patching this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • force (in query): boolean - force apply the patch
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PodDisruptionBudget): OK
  • 201 (PodDisruptionBudget): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Observe Skills Service PDB Status Patches

Note: Status patches are typically performed by Kubernetes controllers automatically.

# Watch Skills PDB status changes
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget skills-api-pdb -n skills-service --watch -o jsonpath='{.status}'
{"currentHealthy":4,"desiredHealthy":2,"disruptionsAllowed":2,"expectedPods":4,"observedGeneration":1}
Example 2: Monitor User Service PDB Status Updates

Track User PDB status as controller updates it.

# Describe User PDB showing controller updates
kubectl describe poddisruptionbudget user-api-pdb -n user-service | grep -A 5 Status:
Status: Allowed disruptions: 1 Current: 3 Desired: 2 Total: 3
Example 3: View Author Service PDB Status Events

Check events related to Author PDB status updates.

# Get Author PDB events
kubectl get events -n author-service --field-selector involvedObject.name=author-api-pdb
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 2m Normal SuccessfulCreate poddisruptionbudget/author-api-pdb Successfully created PDB
Example 4: Check Location Service PDB Status Subresource

View Location PDB status subresource managed by controller.

# Get Location PDB status
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget location-api-pdb -n location-service -o json | jq '.status'
{ "currentHealthy": 3, "desiredHealthy": 2, "disruptionsAllowed": 1, "expectedPods": 3, "observedGeneration": 1 }
Example 5: Monitor JobPost Service PDB Status Conditions

Track JobPost PDB status condition changes by controller.

# Watch JobPost PDB conditions
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget jobpost-api-pdb -n jobpost-service -o yaml | grep -A 20 status:
status: conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-12-15T10:30:00Z" message: "" observedGeneration: 1 reason: SufficientPods status: "True" type: DisruptionAllowed currentHealthy: 5 desiredHealthy: 3 disruptionsAllowed: 2 expectedPods: 5 observedGeneration: 1

delete - delete a PodDisruptionBudget

Delete a specific PodDisruptionBudget from a namespace, removing disruption protection.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/poddisruptionbudgets/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PodDisruptionBudget
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace containing the PDB
  • body: DeleteOptions - deletion options
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period in seconds
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - deletion propagation policy
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 202 (Status): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete Skills Service PDB

Remove PodDisruptionBudget from Skills service namespace.

# Delete Skills PDB
kubectl delete poddisruptionbudget skills-api-pdb -n skills-service
poddisruptionbudget.policy "skills-api-pdb" deleted
Example 2: Delete User Service PDB with Confirmation

Delete User service PDB after verification.

# Verify and delete User PDB
kubectl get poddisruptionbudget user-api-pdb -n user-service
kubectl delete poddisruptionbudget user-api-pdb -n user-service
NAME MIN AVAILABLE MAX UNAVAILABLE ALLOWED DISRUPTIONS AGE user-api-pdb N/A 1 1 5d poddisruptionbudget.policy "user-api-pdb" deleted
Example 3: Delete Author Service PDB with Dry Run

Test deletion of Author PDB without actually removing it.

# Dry run delete Author PDB
kubectl delete poddisruptionbudget author-api-pdb -n author-service --dry-run=server
poddisruptionbudget.policy "author-api-pdb" deleted (server dry run)
Example 4: Delete Location Service PDB from YAML

Delete Location PDB using the original YAML file.

# Delete Location PDB from file
kubectl delete -f location-api-pdb.yaml
poddisruptionbudget.policy "location-api-pdb" deleted
Example 5: Delete JobPost Service PDB Immediately

Remove JobPost PDB without grace period.

# Delete JobPost PDB immediately
kubectl delete poddisruptionbudget jobpost-api-pdb -n jobpost-service --grace-period=0
poddisruptionbudget.policy "jobpost-api-pdb" deleted

deletecollection - delete collection of PodDisruptionBudget

Delete multiple PodDisruptionBudgets in a namespace matching specified criteria.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/poddisruptionbudgets
Parameters
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace to delete PDBs from
  • body: DeleteOptions - deletion options
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter resources
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period in seconds
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter resources
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of resources to delete
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - deletion propagation policy
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete All PDBs in Skills Namespace

Remove all PodDisruptionBudgets from Skills service namespace.

# Delete all PDBs in Skills namespace
kubectl delete poddisruptionbudgets --all -n skills-service
poddisruptionbudget.policy "skills-api-pdb" deleted
Example 2: Delete PDBs by Label in User Service

Delete User service PDBs matching specific label.

# Delete User PDBs with service label
kubectl delete poddisruptionbudgets -l service=user -n user-service
poddisruptionbudget.policy "user-api-pdb" deleted
Example 3: Delete PDBs with Dry Run in Author Service

Preview which Author PDBs would be deleted.

# Dry run delete all PDBs in Author namespace
kubectl delete poddisruptionbudgets --all -n author-service --dry-run=server
poddisruptionbudget.policy "author-api-pdb" deleted (server dry run)
Example 4: Delete PDBs Matching Multiple Labels

Delete Location PDBs with specific label combination.

# Delete Location PDBs matching multiple labels
kubectl delete poddisruptionbudgets -l app=anihpj,tier=backend -n location-service
poddisruptionbudget.policy "location-api-pdb" deleted
Example 5: Delete All anihpj Application PDBs

Remove all PDBs from all anihpj jobpost namespaces.

# Delete PDBs from all anihpj namespaces
for ns in skills-service user-service author-service location-service jobpost-service; do
  kubectl delete poddisruptionbudgets --all -n $ns
done
poddisruptionbudget.policy "skills-api-pdb" deleted poddisruptionbudget.policy "user-api-pdb" deleted poddisruptionbudget.policy "author-api-pdb" deleted poddisruptionbudget.policy "location-api-pdb" deleted poddisruptionbudget.policy "jobpost-api-pdb" deleted

PriorityLevelConfiguration

PriorityLevelConfigurationSpec - Priority level specification
PriorityLevelConfigurationStatus - Priority level status
PriorityLevelConfigurationList - Collection of priority levels
Operations - Manage priority levels

API Group: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1

Official Documentation: PriorityLevelConfiguration v1

Full Description

PriorityLevelConfiguration represents the configuration of a priority level for API Priority and Fairness. It defines how requests are prioritized and limited when accessing the Kubernetes API server, ensuring fair resource distribution and preventing resource exhaustion. In the anihpj jobpost application, priority levels help manage API request priorities for different Django services (Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost), ensuring critical operations get priority during high load.

Priority levels can be either "Limited" (subject to concurrency limits and queuing) or "Exempt" (not subject to limits). Limited priority levels use nominal concurrency shares to determine their portion of API server capacity, with support for borrowing and lending seats between priority levels. This ensures the Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost services maintain appropriate API access priority based on their criticality.

Syntax

# Apply priority level configuration
kubectl apply -f prioritylevel.yaml

# Get priority level
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration

# Describe priority level details
kubectl describe prioritylevelconfiguration 

# Get priority level in YAML
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration  -o yaml

# Get priority level in JSON
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration  -o json

# Delete priority level
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfiguration

YAML Structure

apiVersion: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityLevelConfiguration
metadata:
  name: jobpost-api-priority
spec:
  type: Limited
  limited:
    nominalConcurrencyShares: 50
    limitResponse:
      type: Queue
      queuing:
        queues: 64
        queueLengthLimit: 50
        handSize: 8

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1 - API version
  • kind: PriorityLevelConfiguration - Resource type
  • metadata: ObjectMeta - Standard object metadata
    • name: string - Name of the PriorityLevelConfiguration
    • labels: map[string]string - Key-value pairs for organization
    • annotations: map[string]string - Additional metadata
  • spec: PriorityLevelConfigurationSpec - Priority level specification
    • type: string (required) - "Limited" or "Exempt"
    • limited: LimitedPriorityLevelConfiguration - Configuration for Limited type
      • nominalConcurrencyShares: int32 - Nominal concurrency shares (default: 30)
      • borrowingLimitPercent: int32 - Percentage of borrowing limit
      • lendablePercent: int32 - Percentage that can be lent (0-100)
      • limitResponse: LimitResponse - How to handle exceeded limits
        • type: string (required) - "Queue" or "Reject"
        • queuing: QueuingConfiguration - Queuing parameters
          • queues: int32 - Number of queues (default: 64)
          • queueLengthLimit: int32 - Max requests per queue (default: 50)
          • handSize: int32 - Shuffle sharding hand size (default: 8)
    • exempt: ExemptPriorityLevelConfiguration - Configuration for Exempt type
      • nominalConcurrencyShares: int32 - Nominal concurrency shares
      • lendablePercent: int32 - Percentage that can be lent
  • status: PriorityLevelConfigurationStatus - Current priority level status
    • conditions: []PriorityLevelConfigurationCondition - Status conditions

Return Value

  • 200 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): OK - Request successful
  • 201 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): Created - Resource created
  • 202 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): Accepted - Request accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Service Priority Level

Create high-priority level for Skills API ensuring 60 concurrency shares.

apiVersion: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityLevelConfiguration
metadata:
  name: skills-api-priority
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: skills
spec:
  type: Limited
  limited:
    nominalConcurrencyShares: 60
    lendablePercent: 20
    limitResponse:
      type: Queue
      queuing:
        queues: 128
        queueLengthLimit: 100
        handSize: 8
# Apply Skills priority level
kubectl apply -f skills-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/skills-api-priority created
Example 2: User Service Priority Level

Set up User service with moderate priority and request queuing.

apiVersion: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityLevelConfiguration
metadata:
  name: user-api-priority
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: user
spec:
  type: Limited
  limited:
    nominalConcurrencyShares: 50
    borrowingLimitPercent: 150
    lendablePercent: 30
    limitResponse:
      type: Queue
      queuing:
        queues: 64
        queueLengthLimit: 50
        handSize: 8
# Create User priority level
kubectl create -f user-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/user-api-priority created
Example 3: Author Service Priority Level with Reject

Configure Author service to reject excess requests instead of queuing.

apiVersion: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityLevelConfiguration
metadata:
  name: author-api-priority
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: author
spec:
  type: Limited
  limited:
    nominalConcurrencyShares: 40
    lendablePercent: 25
    limitResponse:
      type: Reject
# Apply Author priority configuration
kubectl apply -f author-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/author-api-priority created
Example 4: Location Service Priority Level

Set Location service with lower priority and borrowing capability.

apiVersion: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityLevelConfiguration
metadata:
  name: location-api-priority
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: location
spec:
  type: Limited
  limited:
    nominalConcurrencyShares: 30
    borrowingLimitPercent: 200
    lendablePercent: 10
    limitResponse:
      type: Queue
      queuing:
        queues: 32
        queueLengthLimit: 30
        handSize: 4
# Create Location priority level
kubectl apply -f location-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/location-api-priority created
Example 5: JobPost Service Exempt Priority Level

Create exempt priority level for critical JobPost operations.

apiVersion: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityLevelConfiguration
metadata:
  name: jobpost-api-critical
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost
    tier: critical
spec:
  type: Exempt
  exempt:
    nominalConcurrencyShares: 100
    lendablePercent: 0
# Apply JobPost critical priority
kubectl apply -f jobpost-critical-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/jobpost-api-critical created

PriorityLevelConfigurationSpec

Full Description

PriorityLevelConfigurationSpec specifies the configuration of a priority level, defining how API requests are prioritized and limited. It determines whether the priority level is "Limited" (subject to concurrency limits and queuing) or "Exempt" (unrestricted). For Limited priority levels, it configures nominal concurrency shares, borrowing/lending percentages, and limit response behavior (Queue or Reject). This specification ensures the Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost Django services get appropriate API priority based on their criticality.

The spec includes LimitedPriorityLevelConfiguration for Limited types, which defines nominalConcurrencyShares (execution seats), borrowingLimitPercent (how much can be borrowed), lendablePercent (how much can be lent), and limitResponse (queuing configuration). For Exempt types, it uses ExemptPriorityLevelConfiguration. These parameters control fair resource distribution across anihpj jobpost application services.

Syntax
# Get spec from priority level
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration  -o jsonpath='{.spec}'

# Extract type
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration  -o jsonpath='{.spec.type}'

# Get nominal concurrency shares
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration  -o jsonpath='{.spec.limited.nominalConcurrencyShares}'
Parameters
  • type: string (required) - "Limited" or "Exempt" - Determines if priority level is subject to limits
  • limited: LimitedPriorityLevelConfiguration - Configuration for Limited priority level
    • nominalConcurrencyShares: int32 - Execution seats (default: 30)
    • borrowingLimitPercent: int32 - Borrowing limit percentage
    • lendablePercent: int32 - Lendable percentage (0-100, default: 0)
    • limitResponse: LimitResponse - Limit handling configuration
      • type: string (required) - "Queue" or "Reject"
      • queuing: QueuingConfiguration - Queuing parameters
        • queues: int32 - Number of queues (default: 64)
        • queueLengthLimit: int32 - Max requests per queue (default: 50)
        • handSize: int32 - Shuffle sharding size (default: 8)
  • exempt: ExemptPriorityLevelConfiguration - Configuration for Exempt priority level
    • nominalConcurrencyShares: int32 - Nominal concurrency shares (default: 0)
    • lendablePercent: int32 - Lendable percentage (0-100, default: 0)
Return Value

Returns PriorityLevelConfigurationSpec object containing the priority level configuration specification with type, limited/exempt settings, and concurrency parameters.

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Skills Service Limited Spec

Extract Skills service priority level spec with Limited configuration.

# Get Skills priority spec
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration skills-api-priority -o jsonpath='{.spec}' | jq
{ "limited": { "lendablePercent": 20, "limitResponse": { "queuing": { "handSize": 8, "queueLengthLimit": 100, "queues": 128 }, "type": "Queue" }, "nominalConcurrencyShares": 60 }, "type": "Limited" }
Example 2: User Service Concurrency Shares

Check User service nominal concurrency shares.

# Get User priority concurrency shares
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration user-api-priority -o jsonpath='{.spec.limited.nominalConcurrencyShares}'
50
Example 3: Author Service Reject Configuration

View Author service spec with Reject limit response.

# Get Author priority spec in YAML
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration author-api-priority -o yaml | grep -A 10 spec:
spec: limited: lendablePercent: 25 limitResponse: type: Reject nominalConcurrencyShares: 40 type: Limited
Example 4: Location Service Borrowing Configuration

Extract Location service borrowing and lending percentages.

# Get Location borrowing and lending config
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration location-api-priority -o json | jq '.spec.limited | {borrowingLimitPercent, lendablePercent}'
{ "borrowingLimitPercent": 200, "lendablePercent": 10 }
Example 5: JobPost Service Exempt Spec

View JobPost critical priority exempt specification.

# Get JobPost exempt spec
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration jobpost-api-critical -o jsonpath='{.spec}' | jq
{ "exempt": { "lendablePercent": 0, "nominalConcurrencyShares": 100 }, "type": "Exempt" }

PriorityLevelConfigurationStatus

Full Description

PriorityLevelConfigurationStatus represents the current state of a priority level configuration. It contains conditions that indicate the operational status of the priority level, such as whether it's been successfully configured or if there are issues. The status provides visibility into the health and state of API priority and fairness controls for the anihpj jobpost application services (Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost).

Each condition in the status includes type, status (True/False/Unknown), reason, message, and lastTransitionTime. This information helps administrators monitor priority level health and troubleshoot any API flow control issues affecting the Django microservices.

Syntax
# Get status from priority level
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration  -o jsonpath='{.status}'

# Check conditions
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration  -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions}'

# Describe to see status
kubectl describe prioritylevelconfiguration  | grep -A 10 Status:
Parameters
  • conditions: []PriorityLevelConfigurationCondition - Array of status conditions
    • type: string (required) - Condition type
    • status: string (required) - "True", "False", or "Unknown"
    • reason: string - Unique CamelCase reason for transition
    • message: string - Human-readable message with details
    • lastTransitionTime: Time - Last time condition transitioned
Return Value

Returns PriorityLevelConfigurationStatus object containing the current status with conditions array indicating priority level health and operational state.

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Skills Service Priority Status

Check Skills service priority level operational status.

# Get Skills priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration skills-api-priority -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq
{ "conditions": [ { "lastTransitionTime": "2025-12-15T10:00:00Z", "message": "Configuration is valid", "reason": "ConfigurationValid", "status": "True", "type": "Ready" } ] }
Example 2: User Service Priority Conditions

View User service priority level conditions.

# Describe User priority to see status
kubectl describe prioritylevelconfiguration user-api-priority | grep -A 15 Status:
Status: Conditions: Last Transition Time: 2025-12-15T10:05:00Z Message: Priority level is functioning normally Reason: Healthy Status: True Type: Ready Events:
Example 3: Author Service Priority Health Check

Monitor Author service priority level health status.

# Get Author priority conditions
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration author-api-priority -o yaml | grep -A 10 status:
status: conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-12-15T10:10:00Z" message: "" reason: ConfigurationValid status: "True" type: Ready
Example 4: Location Service Status Monitoring

Track Location service priority level status over time.

# Watch Location priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration location-api-priority --watch -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[0].status}'
True
Example 5: JobPost Service Exempt Status

Verify JobPost critical priority exempt status is healthy.

# Get JobPost priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration jobpost-api-critical -o json | jq '.status'
{ "conditions": [ { "lastTransitionTime": "2025-12-15T10:15:00Z", "message": "Exempt priority level configured successfully", "reason": "ExemptConfigured", "status": "True", "type": "Ready" } ] }

PriorityLevelConfigurationList

Full Description

PriorityLevelConfigurationList is a collection of PriorityLevelConfiguration objects used for listing and managing multiple priority levels in the cluster. It contains an array of priority level items and metadata for the collection. This resource type is returned when listing all priority levels, allowing administrators to review API priority and fairness configurations across all anihpj jobpost application services (Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost).

The list includes metadata (like resource version and continue token for pagination) and an items array containing all PriorityLevelConfiguration objects that match the query. This enables batch operations, auditing, and comprehensive monitoring of API flow control settings affecting the Django microservices.

Syntax
# List all priority levels
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations

# List with custom columns
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,TYPE:.spec.type,SHARES:.spec.limited.nominalConcurrencyShares

# List in JSON format
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -o json
Parameters
  • apiVersion: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1 - API version
  • kind: PriorityLevelConfigurationList - Resource type
  • metadata: ListMeta - Standard list metadata
    • resourceVersion: string - Version of the resource list
    • continue: string - Token for pagination
    • remainingItemCount: int64 - Remaining items in pagination
  • items: []PriorityLevelConfiguration (required) - Array of PriorityLevelConfiguration objects
Return Value

Returns PriorityLevelConfigurationList object containing metadata and an array of PriorityLevelConfiguration items representing all priority levels in the cluster.

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Priority Levels

View all priority levels for anihpj Django services.

# List all priority levels with label
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj
NAME TYPE AGE skills-api-priority Limited 5d user-api-priority Limited 5d author-api-priority Limited 5d location-api-priority Limited 5d jobpost-api-critical Exempt 5d
Example 2: List Priority Levels with Custom Columns

Display priority levels with type and concurrency shares.

# List with custom output
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,TYPE:.spec.type,SHARES:.spec.limited.nominalConcurrencyShares
NAME TYPE SHARES skills-api-priority Limited 60 user-api-priority Limited 50 author-api-priority Limited 40 location-api-priority Limited 30 jobpost-api-critical Exempt
Example 3: List Priority Levels in JSON Format

Extract priority level list as JSON for processing.

# Get priority list in JSON
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, type: .spec.type}'
{ "name": "skills-api-priority", "type": "Limited" } { "name": "user-api-priority", "type": "Limited" }
Example 4: Count Total Priority Levels

Count how many priority levels exist for anihpj services.

# Count priority levels
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj --no-headers | wc -l
5
Example 5: List Priority Levels Wide Format

View priority levels with additional details.

# List in wide format
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj -o wide
NAME TYPE AGE skills-api-priority Limited 5d user-api-priority Limited 5d author-api-priority Limited 5d location-api-priority Limited 5d jobpost-api-critical Exempt 5d

Operations

The following operations are available for managing PriorityLevelConfiguration resources in the anihpj jobpost application for controlling API priority and fairness across Django services.

get - Read specified PriorityLevelConfiguration
get-status - Read status of PriorityLevelConfiguration
list - List all PriorityLevelConfigurations
create - Create a PriorityLevelConfiguration
update - Replace PriorityLevelConfiguration
update-status - Replace PriorityLevelConfiguration status
patch - Partially update PriorityLevelConfiguration
patch-status - Partially update status
delete - Delete a PriorityLevelConfiguration
deletecollection - Delete multiple PriorityLevelConfigurations

get - read the specified PriorityLevelConfiguration

Retrieve detailed information about a specific PriorityLevelConfiguration.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1/prioritylevelconfigurations/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PriorityLevelConfiguration
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Skills Service Priority Level

Retrieve Skills service priority configuration in YAML format.

# Get Skills priority level
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration skills-api-priority -o yaml
apiVersion: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1 kind: PriorityLevelConfiguration metadata: labels: app: anihpj service: skills name: skills-api-priority spec: limited: lendablePercent: 20 limitResponse: queuing: handSize: 8 queueLengthLimit: 100 queues: 128 type: Queue nominalConcurrencyShares: 60 type: Limited
Example 2: Get User Service Priority with Describe

View detailed User service priority information.

# Describe User priority level
kubectl describe prioritylevelconfiguration user-api-priority
Name: user-api-priority API Version: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1 Kind: PriorityLevelConfiguration Metadata: Labels: app: anihpj service: user Spec: Limited: Borrowing Limit Percent: 150 Lendable Percent: 30 Limit Response: Queuing: Hand Size: 8 Queue Length Limit: 50 Queues: 64 Type: Queue Nominal Concurrency Shares: 50 Type: Limited Status: Conditions: Status: True Type: Ready Events:
Example 3: Get Author Service Priority in JSON

Retrieve Author service priority as JSON.

# Get Author priority in JSON
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration author-api-priority -o json
{ "apiVersion": "flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1", "kind": "PriorityLevelConfiguration", "metadata": { "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "service": "author" }, "name": "author-api-priority" }, "spec": { "limited": { "lendablePercent": 25, "limitResponse": { "type": "Reject" }, "nominalConcurrencyShares": 40 }, "type": "Limited" } }
Example 4: Get Location Service Priority Type

Extract specific field from Location service priority.

# Get Location priority type
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration location-api-priority -o jsonpath='{.spec.type}'
Limited
Example 5: Get JobPost Service Exempt Priority

View JobPost critical exempt priority configuration.

# Get JobPost exempt priority
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration jobpost-api-critical -o yaml
apiVersion: flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1 kind: PriorityLevelConfiguration metadata: labels: app: anihpj service: jobpost tier: critical name: jobpost-api-critical spec: exempt: lendablePercent: 0 nominalConcurrencyShares: 100 type: Exempt

get - read status of the specified PriorityLevelConfiguration

Retrieve status information for a specific PriorityLevelConfiguration.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1/prioritylevelconfigurations/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PriorityLevelConfiguration
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Skills Service Priority Status

Check Skills service priority level status.

# Get Skills priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration skills-api-priority -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq
{ "conditions": [ { "lastTransitionTime": "2025-12-15T10:00:00Z", "message": "Configuration is valid", "reason": "ConfigurationValid", "status": "True", "type": "Ready" } ] }
Example 2: Check User Service Priority Conditions

View User service priority level conditions.

# Check User priority conditions
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration user-api-priority -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions}'
[{"lastTransitionTime":"2025-12-15T10:05:00Z","message":"Priority level is functioning normally","reason":"Healthy","status":"True","type":"Ready"}]
Example 3: Get Author Service Priority Health

Monitor Author service priority level health.

# Describe Author priority status
kubectl describe prioritylevelconfiguration author-api-priority | grep -A 10 Status:
Status: Conditions: Last Transition Time: 2025-12-15T10:10:00Z Message: Reason: ConfigurationValid Status: True Type: Ready Events:
Example 4: Monitor Location Service Status

Track Location service priority level status.

# Get Location priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration location-api-priority -o yaml | grep -A 10 status:
status: conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-12-15T10:12:00Z" message: "" reason: Ready status: "True" type: Ready
Example 5: Get JobPost Service Exempt Status

Verify JobPost critical priority status.

# Get JobPost priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration jobpost-api-critical -o json | jq '.status'
{ "conditions": [ { "lastTransitionTime": "2025-12-15T10:15:00Z", "message": "Exempt priority level configured successfully", "reason": "ExemptConfigured", "status": "True", "type": "Ready" } ] }

list - list or watch objects of kind PriorityLevelConfiguration

List all PriorityLevelConfigurations in the cluster with optional filtering.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1/prioritylevelconfigurations
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - allow watch bookmarks
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter results
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter results
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (PriorityLevelConfigurationList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Priority Levels

Get all priority levels for anihpj services.

# List all anihpj priority levels
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj
NAME TYPE AGE skills-api-priority Limited 5d user-api-priority Limited 5d author-api-priority Limited 5d location-api-priority Limited 5d jobpost-api-critical Exempt 5d
Example 2: List Priority Levels with Labels

Filter priority levels by service label.

# List with service label
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -l service=skills
NAME TYPE AGE skills-api-priority Limited 5d
Example 3: List Priority Levels with Custom Columns

Display priority levels with specific information.

# List with custom columns
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,TYPE:.spec.type,SHARES:.spec.limited.nominalConcurrencyShares
NAME TYPE SHARES skills-api-priority Limited 60 user-api-priority Limited 50 author-api-priority Limited 40 location-api-priority Limited 30 jobpost-api-critical Exempt
Example 4: Watch Priority Levels

Monitor changes to priority levels in real-time.

# Watch priority levels
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj --watch
NAME TYPE AGE skills-api-priority Limited 5d user-api-priority Limited 5d
Example 5: List Priority Levels in JSON

Get priority levels as JSON for processing.

# List in JSON format
kubectl get prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, type: .spec.type}'
{ "name": "skills-api-priority", "type": "Limited" } { "name": "user-api-priority", "type": "Limited" }

create - create a PriorityLevelConfiguration

Create a new PriorityLevelConfiguration to control API request priorities.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1/prioritylevelconfigurations
Parameters
  • body: PriorityLevelConfiguration, required - PriorityLevelConfiguration object to create
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager creating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): OK
  • 201 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): Created
  • 202 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Create Skills Service Priority Level

Create high-priority level for Skills API.

# Create Skills priority level
kubectl apply -f skills-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/skills-api-priority created
Example 2: Create User Service Priority Level

Set up User service priority with queuing.

# Create User priority level
kubectl create -f user-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/user-api-priority created
Example 3: Create Author Service Priority with Dry Run

Test Author service priority creation without applying.

# Create Author priority with dry-run
kubectl apply -f author-priority.yaml --dry-run=server
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/author-api-priority created (server dry run)
Example 4: Create Location Service Priority Level

Create Location service priority with borrowing capability.

# Apply Location priority
kubectl apply -f location-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/location-api-priority created
Example 5: Create JobPost Service Exempt Priority

Create exempt priority for critical JobPost operations.

# Create JobPost exempt priority
kubectl apply -f jobpost-critical-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/jobpost-api-critical created

update - replace the specified PriorityLevelConfiguration

Replace an existing PriorityLevelConfiguration with a new definition.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1/prioritylevelconfigurations/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PriorityLevelConfiguration
  • body: PriorityLevelConfiguration, required - updated PriorityLevelConfiguration object
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager updating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): OK
  • 201 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Update Skills Service Concurrency Shares

Increase Skills priority level concurrency shares.

# Edit and update Skills priority
kubectl edit prioritylevelconfiguration skills-api-priority
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/skills-api-priority edited
Example 2: Update User Service Queue Configuration

Modify User service queuing parameters.

# Get, modify, and apply User priority
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration user-api-priority -o yaml > user-priority.yaml
# Edit user-priority.yaml
kubectl apply -f user-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/user-api-priority configured
Example 3: Update Author Service Limit Response

Change Author service from Reject to Queue.

# Replace Author priority
kubectl replace -f author-priority-updated.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/author-api-priority replaced
Example 4: Update Location Service Borrowing Limit

Adjust Location service borrowing percentage.

# Update Location priority with dry-run
kubectl apply -f location-priority-v2.yaml --dry-run=server
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/location-api-priority configured (server dry run)
Example 5: Update JobPost Service Exempt Configuration

Modify JobPost exempt priority settings.

# Apply updated JobPost priority
kubectl apply -f jobpost-critical-priority-v2.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/jobpost-api-critical configured

update - replace status of the specified PriorityLevelConfiguration

Replace the status subresource of an existing PriorityLevelConfiguration. Note: Status is typically managed by the controller.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1/prioritylevelconfigurations/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PriorityLevelConfiguration
  • body: PriorityLevelConfiguration, required - updated PriorityLevelConfiguration object
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager updating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): OK
  • 201 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: View Skills Service Priority Status

Check Skills priority level status (typically managed by controller).

# View Skills priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration skills-api-priority -o jsonpath='{.status}'
{"conditions":[{"lastTransitionTime":"2025-12-15T10:00:00Z","message":"Configuration is valid","reason":"ConfigurationValid","status":"True","type":"Ready"}]}
Example 2: Monitor User Service Priority Conditions

Observe User priority level health status.

# Check User priority conditions
kubectl describe prioritylevelconfiguration user-api-priority | grep -A 5 Conditions:
Conditions: Last Transition Time: 2025-12-15T10:05:00Z Message: Priority level is functioning normally Reason: Healthy Status: True Type: Ready
Example 3: Check Author Service Priority Health

View Author priority level operational status.

# Get Author priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration author-api-priority -o yaml | grep -A 10 status:
status: conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-12-15T10:10:00Z" message: "" reason: ConfigurationValid status: "True" type: Ready
Example 4: Monitor Location Service Status

Track Location priority level conditions.

# Watch Location priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration location-api-priority -w -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*].status}'
True
Example 5: Verify JobPost Service Exempt Status

Confirm JobPost critical priority status.

# Get JobPost priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration jobpost-api-critical -o json | jq '.status'
{ "conditions": [ { "lastTransitionTime": "2025-12-15T10:15:00Z", "message": "Exempt priority level configured successfully", "reason": "ExemptConfigured", "status": "True", "type": "Ready" } ] }

patch - partially update the specified PriorityLevelConfiguration

Partially update a PriorityLevelConfiguration using JSON patch, merge patch, or strategic merge patch.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1/prioritylevelconfigurations/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PriorityLevelConfiguration
  • body: Patch, required - patch to apply
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager patching this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • force (in query): boolean - force apply the patch
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): OK
  • 201 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Patch Skills Service Concurrency Shares

Update Skills priority level shares using JSON patch.

# Patch Skills nominalConcurrencyShares
kubectl patch prioritylevelconfiguration skills-api-priority --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/limited/nominalConcurrencyShares", "value": 70}]'
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/skills-api-priority patched
Example 2: Patch User Service Labels

Add or update User priority level labels.

# Patch User priority labels
kubectl patch prioritylevelconfiguration user-api-priority --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"tier":"standard","env":"production"}}}'
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/user-api-priority patched
Example 3: Patch Author Service Queue Length

Modify Author priority queuing configuration.

# Patch Author queue configuration
kubectl patch prioritylevelconfiguration author-api-priority --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"limited":{"limitResponse":{"queuing":{"queueLengthLimit":200}}}}}'
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/author-api-priority patched
Example 4: Patch Location Service Borrowing Limit

Adjust Location priority borrowing percentage.

# Patch Location borrowingLimitPercent
kubectl patch prioritylevelconfiguration location-api-priority --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/limited/borrowingLimitPercent", "value": 250}]'
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/location-api-priority patched
Example 5: Patch JobPost Service Multiple Fields

Update JobPost priority with multiple changes.

# Patch JobPost priority
kubectl patch prioritylevelconfiguration jobpost-api-critical --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"annotations":{"description":"Critical operations"}}, "spec":{"exempt":{"nominalConcurrencyShares":120}}}'
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/jobpost-api-critical patched

patch - partially update status of the specified PriorityLevelConfiguration

Partially update the status subresource of a PriorityLevelConfiguration. Note: Status is typically managed by the controller.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1/prioritylevelconfigurations/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PriorityLevelConfiguration
  • body: Patch, required - patch to apply
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager patching this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • force (in query): boolean - force apply the patch
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): OK
  • 201 (PriorityLevelConfiguration): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: View Skills Service Priority Status

Monitor Skills priority level status (controller-managed).

# Check Skills priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration skills-api-priority -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*].type}'
Ready
Example 2: Check User Service Priority Conditions

View User priority level conditions.

# Get User priority conditions
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration user-api-priority -o json | jq '.status.conditions'
[ { "lastTransitionTime": "2025-12-15T10:05:00Z", "message": "Priority level is functioning normally", "reason": "Healthy", "status": "True", "type": "Ready" } ]
Example 3: Monitor Author Service Priority Health

Track Author priority level status.

# Describe Author priority status
kubectl describe prioritylevelconfiguration author-api-priority | grep -A 6 Status:
Status: Conditions: Last Transition Time: 2025-12-15T10:10:00Z Message: Reason: ConfigurationValid Status: True Type: Ready
Example 4: Check Location Service Status

Verify Location priority level operational state.

# Get Location priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration location-api-priority -o yaml | grep -A 8 status:
status: conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-12-15T10:12:00Z" message: "" reason: Ready status: "True" type: Ready
Example 5: Monitor JobPost Service Exempt Status

Track JobPost critical priority health.

# Get JobPost priority status
kubectl get prioritylevelconfiguration jobpost-api-critical -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[0]}'
{"lastTransitionTime":"2025-12-15T10:15:00Z","message":"Exempt priority level configured successfully","reason":"ExemptConfigured","status":"True","type":"Ready"}

delete - delete a PriorityLevelConfiguration

Delete a specific PriorityLevelConfiguration resource.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1/prioritylevelconfigurations/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the PriorityLevelConfiguration
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period before deletion
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy (Orphan, Background, Foreground)
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 202 (Status): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete Skills Service Priority Level

Remove Skills priority level configuration.

# Delete Skills priority
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfiguration skills-api-priority
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "skills-api-priority" deleted
Example 2: Delete User Service Priority with Confirmation

Delete User priority level with explicit confirmation.

# Delete User priority
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfiguration user-api-priority --wait=true
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "user-api-priority" deleted
Example 3: Delete Author Service Priority with Dry Run

Test Author priority deletion without applying.

# Dry-run delete Author priority
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfiguration author-api-priority --dry-run=client
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "author-api-priority" deleted (dry run)
Example 4: Delete Location Service Priority Immediately

Force immediate deletion of Location priority.

# Force delete Location priority
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfiguration location-api-priority --grace-period=0 --force
warning: Immediate deletion does not wait for confirmation that the running resource has been terminated. The resource may continue to run on the cluster indefinitely. prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "location-api-priority" force deleted
Example 5: Delete JobPost Service Priority from YAML

Remove JobPost critical priority using YAML file.

# Delete JobPost priority from file
kubectl delete -f jobpost-critical-priority.yaml
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "jobpost-api-critical" deleted

deletecollection - delete collection of PriorityLevelConfiguration

Delete multiple PriorityLevelConfigurations matching specified criteria.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io/v1/prioritylevelconfigurations
Parameters
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter resources
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period before deletion
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter resources
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of resources to delete
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy (Orphan, Background, Foreground)
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete All anihpj Priority Levels

Remove all priority levels with app label.

# Delete all anihpj priority levels
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "skills-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "user-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "author-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "location-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "jobpost-api-critical" deleted
Example 2: Delete Limited Type Priority Levels

Remove all Limited type priority levels for specific service.

# Delete Skills service priorities
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfigurations -l service=skills
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "skills-api-priority" deleted
Example 3: Delete Priority Levels with Dry Run

Test deletion of User service priorities.

# Dry-run delete User priorities
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfigurations -l service=user --dry-run=client
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "user-api-priority" deleted (dry run)
Example 4: Delete Multiple Priority Levels by Label

Remove Author and Location priority levels.

# Delete multiple services
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfigurations -l 'service in (author,location)'
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "author-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "location-api-priority" deleted
Example 5: Delete Non-Exempt Priority Levels

Remove all anihpj priorities except exempt tier.

# Delete all anihpj priorities except critical
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj,tier!=critical
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "skills-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "user-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "author-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "location-api-priority" deleted

ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

📋 ValidatingAdmissionPolicy Overview

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyList - Collection of admission policies
