# Get list in JSON format for processing
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -o json

Parameters

  • apiVersion (string): API version - admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
  • kind (string): Resource type - MutatingWebhookConfigurationList
  • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata with continue token, resource version, remaining item count
  • items ([]MutatingWebhookConfiguration, required): Array of MutatingWebhookConfiguration objects in the list

Return Value

Returns a MutatingWebhookConfigurationList object containing matching webhook configurations with metadata for pagination and an array of items.

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: List All anihpj Webhook Configurations

This example demonstrates listing all mutating webhook configurations for the anihpj application across all models.

# List all anihpj webhook configurations
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj

# Show detailed output with labels
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj --show-labels
NAME WEBHOOKS AGE anihpj-author-label-injector 1 5d anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer 1 3d anihpj-location-network-injector 1 7d anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector 1 10d anihpj-user-resource-enforcer 1 8d
Example 2: List with Custom Columns for User Model

This example shows listing webhook configurations with custom columns to display specific information about User model webhooks.

# List User model webhooks with custom columns
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l model=User \
  -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,MODEL:.metadata.labels.model,PURPOSE:.metadata.labels.purpose,FAILURE-POLICY:.webhooks[0].failurePolicy,TIMEOUT:.webhooks[0].timeoutSeconds
NAME MODEL PURPOSE FAILURE-POLICY TIMEOUT anihpj-user-resource-enforcer User resource-enforcement Fail 10
Example 3: Count Total Webhook Configurations

This example demonstrates counting all webhook configurations in the cluster for monitoring purposes.

# Count all webhook configurations
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations --no-headers | wc -l

# Count anihpj webhook configurations by model
for model in Skills User Author Location JobPost; do
  count=$(kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj,model=$model --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
  echo "Model $model: $count webhook(s)"
done
5 Model Skills: 1 webhook(s) Model User: 1 webhook(s) Model Author: 1 webhook(s) Model Location: 1 webhook(s) Model JobPost: 1 webhook(s)
Example 4: Watch Webhook Configuration Changes

This example shows watching for real-time changes to webhook configurations for monitoring and alerting.

# Watch all webhook configuration changes
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations --watch

# Watch only anihpj webhook configurations with timestamp
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj --watch-only
NAME WEBHOOKS AGE anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector 1 10d anihpj-user-resource-enforcer 1 8d anihpj-author-label-injector 1 5d (MODIFIED) anihpj-location-network-injector 1 7d anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer 1 3d (ADDED)
Example 5: Extract Webhook Names for All Models

This example demonstrates extracting webhook names from configurations for automation and scripting.

# Extract all webhook names using JSONPath
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj \
  -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.webhooks[0].name}{"\n"}{end}'

# Get webhook service endpoints
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj \
  -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.webhooks[0].clientConfig.service.name}{"."}  {.webhooks[0].clientConfig.service.namespace}{"\n"}{end}'
anihpj-author-label-injector author-metadata.anihpj.svc anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer jobpost-security.anihpj.svc anihpj-location-network-injector location-network.anihpj.svc anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector skills-sidecar.anihpj.svc anihpj-user-resource-enforcer user-resources.anihpj.svc anihpj-author-label-injector anihpj-webhook-service.anihpj-system anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer anihpj-webhook-service.anihpj-system anihpj-location-network-injector anihpj-webhook-service.anihpj-system anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector anihpj-webhook-service.anihpj-system anihpj-user-resource-enforcer anihpj-webhook-service.anihpj-system

Operations

The following operations are available for managing MutatingWebhookConfiguration resources in your anihpj jobpost application for configuring admission webhooks, controlling object mutations, and enforcing organizational policies.

get - Read specified MutatingWebhookConfiguration
list - List or watch MutatingWebhookConfigurations
create - Create MutatingWebhookConfiguration
update - Replace MutatingWebhookConfiguration
patch - Partially update MutatingWebhookConfiguration
delete - Delete MutatingWebhookConfiguration
deletecollection - Delete collection of MutatingWebhookConfigurations

get - read the specified MutatingWebhookConfiguration

Retrieve detailed information about a specific MutatingWebhookConfiguration including webhook rules, client configuration, selectors, and admission policies.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/mutatingwebhookconfigurations/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path, string, required): Name of the MutatingWebhookConfiguration to retrieve
  • pretty (in query, string): If 'true', prettify the JSON output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingWebhookConfiguration): OK - Request successful, configuration returned
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Skills Sidecar Injector Configuration in YAML

Retrieve the Skills model sidecar injection webhook configuration for inspection and debugging.

# Get Skills sidecar injector webhook configuration
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector -o yaml
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1 kind: MutatingWebhookConfiguration metadata: name: anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector labels: app: anihpj model: Skills webhooks: - name: skills-sidecar.anihpj.svc clientConfig: service: name: anihpj-webhook-service namespace: anihpj-system rules: - operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"] resources: ["pods"]
Example 2: Describe User Resource Enforcer Webhook

Get detailed human-readable information about the User model resource enforcement webhook.

# Describe User resource enforcer webhook
kubectl describe mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-user-resource-enforcer
Name: anihpj-user-resource-enforcer Namespace: Labels: app=anihpj model=User purpose=resource-enforcement API Version: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1 Kind: MutatingWebhookConfiguration Webhooks: Name: user-resources.anihpj.svc Admission Review Versions: v1 Client Config: Service: Name: anihpj-webhook-service Namespace: anihpj-system Path: /enforce-user-resources Port: 443 Failure Policy: Fail Rules: Operations: CREATE Resources: deployments statefulsets
Example 3: Get Author Label Injector in JSON

Retrieve the Author model metadata injection webhook in JSON format for programmatic processing.

# Get Author label injector in JSON format
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-author-label-injector -o json | jq '.webhooks[0] | {name, failurePolicy, timeoutSeconds}'
{ "name": "author-metadata.anihpj.svc", "failurePolicy": "Ignore", "timeoutSeconds": 5 }
Example 4: Extract Location Network Injector Selectors

Get the namespace and object selectors from the Location model network policy injection webhook.

# Extract selectors from Location network injector
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-location-network-injector \
  -o jsonpath='{.webhooks[0].namespaceSelector}{"\n"}{.webhooks[0].objectSelector}' | jq
{ "matchLabels": { "app": "anihpj", "security-zone": "restricted" } } { "matchExpressions": [ { "key": "model", "operator": "In", "values": ["Location"] } ] }
Example 5: Get JobPost Security Enforcer with Labels

Retrieve the JobPost model security enforcement webhook showing labels for categorization.

# Get JobPost security enforcer with labels
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer --show-labels

# Get specific label value
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer -o jsonpath='{.metadata.labels.purpose}'
NAME WEBHOOKS AGE LABELS anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer 1 3d app=anihpj,model=JobPost,purpose=security-enforcement security-enforcement

list - list or watch objects of kind MutatingWebhookConfiguration

List all MutatingWebhookConfiguration objects cluster-wide with support for filtering, pagination, and real-time watching.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/mutatingwebhookconfigurations
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query, boolean): Request watch bookmarks for efficient resumption
  • continue (in query, string): Token for retrieving next page of results
  • fieldSelector (in query, string): Filter by field (e.g., metadata.name=anihpj-skills)
  • labelSelector (in query, string): Filter by labels (e.g., app=anihpj,model=Skills)
  • limit (in query, integer): Maximum number of results to return
  • pretty (in query, string): If 'true', prettify the JSON output
  • resourceVersion (in query, string): Resource version for list consistency
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query, string): How to match resource version (NotOlderThan, Exact)
  • sendInitialEvents (in query, boolean): Send synthetic events for initial state
  • timeoutSeconds (in query, integer): Timeout for list/watch operation
  • watch (in query, boolean): Watch for changes to the requested objects
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingWebhookConfigurationList): OK - List of configurations returned
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Webhook Configurations

List all mutating webhook configurations for the anihpj Django application.

# List all anihpj webhook configurations
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj
NAME WEBHOOKS AGE anihpj-author-label-injector 1 5d anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer 1 3d anihpj-location-network-injector 1 7d anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector 1 10d anihpj-user-resource-enforcer 1 8d
Example 2: List with Custom Columns for All Models

Display webhook configurations with custom columns showing model, purpose, and failure policy.

# List with custom columns
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj \
  -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,MODEL:.metadata.labels.model,PURPOSE:.metadata.labels.purpose,WEBHOOKS:.webhooks[*].name,FAILURE:.webhooks[0].failurePolicy
NAME MODEL PURPOSE WEBHOOKS FAILURE anihpj-author-label-injector Author metadata-injection author-metadata.anihpj.svc Ignore anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer JobPost security-enforcement jobpost-security.anihpj.svc Fail anihpj-location-network-injector Location network-security location-network.anihpj.svc Fail anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector Skills sidecar-injection skills-sidecar.anihpj.svc Fail anihpj-user-resource-enforcer User resource-enforcement user-resources.anihpj.svc Fail
Example 3: Watch for Webhook Configuration Changes

Monitor webhook configuration changes in real-time for operational awareness.

# Watch webhook configuration changes
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj --watch
NAME WEBHOOKS AGE anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector 1 10d anihpj-user-resource-enforcer 1 8d anihpj-author-label-injector 1 5d anihpj-author-label-injector 1 5d (MODIFIED) anihpj-location-network-injector 1 7d anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer 1 3d
Example 4: Extract All Webhook Service Names

Get the service names and namespaces for all webhook endpoints for verification.

# Extract webhook service endpoints
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj \
  -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.webhooks[0].clientConfig.service.name}{"."}{.webhooks[0].clientConfig.service.namespace}{"\n"}{end}'
anihpj-author-label-injector anihpj-webhook-service.anihpj-system anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer anihpj-webhook-service.anihpj-system anihpj-location-network-injector anihpj-webhook-service.anihpj-system anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector anihpj-webhook-service.anihpj-system anihpj-user-resource-enforcer anihpj-webhook-service.anihpj-system
Example 5: Filter Webhooks by Failure Policy

Find all webhook configurations with strict failure policy for critical admission control.

# List webhooks with Fail policy (using client-side filtering)
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj -o json | \
  jq -r '.items[] | select(.webhooks[0].failurePolicy=="Fail") | .metadata.name'
anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer anihpj-location-network-injector anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector anihpj-user-resource-enforcer

create - create a MutatingWebhookConfiguration

Create a new MutatingWebhookConfiguration to register admission webhooks for intercepting and modifying API requests.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/mutatingwebhookconfigurations
Parameters
  • body (MutatingWebhookConfiguration, required): The webhook configuration to create
  • dryRun (in query, string): Simulate creation without persisting (All, None)
  • fieldManager (in query, string): Manager name for tracking field ownership
  • fieldValidation (in query, string): Field validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query, string): If 'true', prettify the JSON output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingWebhookConfiguration): OK - Configuration already exists
  • 201 (MutatingWebhookConfiguration): Created - Configuration successfully created
  • 202 (MutatingWebhookConfiguration): Accepted - Request accepted for processing
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Create Skills Sidecar Injection Webhook

Create a webhook to automatically inject monitoring sidecars into Skills training pods.

# Create Skills sidecar injection webhook
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: MutatingWebhookConfiguration
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: Skills
webhooks:
- name: skills-sidecar.anihpj.svc
  clientConfig:
    service:
      name: anihpj-webhook-service
      namespace: anihpj-system
      path: "/inject-skills-sidecar"
      port: 443
    caBundle: LS0tLS1CRUdJTi...
  rules:
  - operations: ["CREATE"]
    apiGroups: [""]
    apiVersions: ["v1"]
    resources: ["pods"]
  failurePolicy: Fail
  sideEffects: None
  admissionReviewVersions: ["v1"]
EOF
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector created
Example 2: Create User Resource Enforcement Webhook

Create a webhook to enforce resource limits on User data processing workloads.

# Create User resource enforcement webhook
kubectl apply -f user-resource-enforcer.yaml
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-user-resource-enforcer created
Example 3: Create Author Metadata Injection Webhook with Dry Run

Test webhook creation for Author label/annotation injection without actually creating it.

# Create Author metadata webhook with dry-run
kubectl apply -f author-label-injector.yaml --dry-run=server
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-author-label-injector created (server dry run)
Example 4: Create Location Network Policy Webhook

Create a webhook to inject network policy configurations into Location geospatial pods.

# Create Location network policy injection webhook
cat location-network-injector.yaml | kubectl create -f -
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-location-network-injector created
Example 5: Create JobPost Security Enforcement Webhook

Create a comprehensive webhook to enforce security policies on JobPost analytics workloads.

# Create JobPost security enforcement webhook
kubectl create -f jobpost-security-enforcer.yaml --field-manager=anihpj-admin
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer created

update - replace the specified MutatingWebhookConfiguration

Replace an entire MutatingWebhookConfiguration with a new definition, updating all webhook rules and configurations.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/mutatingwebhookconfigurations/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path, string, required): Name of the MutatingWebhookConfiguration to update
  • body (MutatingWebhookConfiguration, required): The updated webhook configuration
  • dryRun (in query, string): Simulate update without persisting (All, None)
  • fieldManager (in query, string): Manager name for tracking field ownership
  • fieldValidation (in query, string): Field validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query, string): If 'true', prettify the JSON output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingWebhookConfiguration): OK - Configuration successfully updated
  • 201 (MutatingWebhookConfiguration): Created - Configuration created (if not existing)
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Update Skills Webhook Rules

Update the Skills sidecar injection webhook to include UPDATE operations in addition to CREATE.

# Update Skills webhook to include UPDATE operations
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector -o yaml > /tmp/skills-webhook.yaml
# Edit the rules to include UPDATE operation
sed -i 's/operations: \["CREATE"\]/operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]/' /tmp/skills-webhook.yaml
kubectl replace -f /tmp/skills-webhook.yaml
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector replaced
Example 2: Update User Webhook Timeout

Increase the timeout for User resource enforcement webhook from 10 to 20 seconds.

# Update User webhook timeout
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-user-resource-enforcer -o yaml | \
  sed 's/timeoutSeconds: 10/timeoutSeconds: 20/' | \
  kubectl replace -f -
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-user-resource-enforcer replaced
Example 3: Update Author Webhook with Dry Run

Test updating the Author metadata injection webhook configuration without actually applying changes.

# Update Author webhook with dry-run
kubectl replace -f author-label-injector-updated.yaml --dry-run=server
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-author-label-injector replaced (server dry run)
Example 4: Update Location Webhook Failure Policy

Change the Location network injection webhook failure policy from Fail to Ignore for resilience.

# Update Location webhook failure policy
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-location-network-injector -o json | \
  jq '.webhooks[0].failurePolicy = "Ignore"' | \
  kubectl replace -f -
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-location-network-injector replaced
Example 5: Update JobPost Webhook Namespace Selector

Update the JobPost security enforcement webhook to target additional namespaces.

# Update JobPost webhook namespace selector
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: MutatingWebhookConfiguration
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer
spec:
  webhooks:
  - name: jobpost-security.anihpj.svc
    namespaceSelector:
      matchExpressions:
      - key: environment
        operator: In
        values: ["production", "staging", "qa"]
    # ... rest of configuration
EOF
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer configured

patch - partially update the specified MutatingWebhookConfiguration

Partially update a MutatingWebhookConfiguration by modifying specific fields without replacing the entire object.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/mutatingwebhookconfigurations/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path, string, required): Name of the MutatingWebhookConfiguration to patch
  • body (Patch, required): The patch to apply (JSON Patch, Merge Patch, or Strategic Merge Patch)
  • dryRun (in query, string): Simulate patch without persisting (All, None)
  • fieldManager (in query, string): Manager name for tracking field ownership
  • fieldValidation (in query, string): Field validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • force (in query, boolean): Force apply patch, overriding conflicts
  • pretty (in query, string): If 'true', prettify the JSON output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingWebhookConfiguration): OK - Configuration successfully patched
  • 201 (MutatingWebhookConfiguration): Created - Configuration created (if not existing)
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Patch Skills Webhook Labels

Add new labels to the Skills sidecar injection webhook for better categorization.

# Patch Skills webhook to add labels
kubectl patch mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector --type merge -p '
{
  "metadata": {
    "labels": {
      "version": "v2.1",
      "team": "ml-platform"
    }
  }
}'
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector patched
Example 2: Patch User Webhook Annotation

Add operational annotations to the User resource enforcement webhook for monitoring.

# Add annotation to User webhook
kubectl annotate mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-user-resource-enforcer \
  monitoring.anihpj.com/alert-threshold="80" \
  ops.anihpj.com/owner="data-platform-team"
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-user-resource-enforcer annotated
Example 3: Patch Author Webhook CA Bundle

Update the CA bundle for the Author metadata injection webhook after certificate rotation.

# Patch Author webhook CA bundle
NEW_CA_BUNDLE=$(cat new-ca-cert.pem | base64 -w 0)
kubectl patch mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-author-label-injector --type='json' -p="[
  {\"op\": \"replace\", \"path\": \"/webhooks/0/clientConfig/caBundle\", \"value\": \"$NEW_CA_BUNDLE\"}
]"
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-author-label-injector patched
Example 4: Patch Location Webhook with JSON Patch

Use JSON Patch to add a new match condition to the Location network injection webhook.

# Add match condition using JSON Patch
kubectl patch mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-location-network-injector --type='json' -p='[
  {
    "op": "add",
    "path": "/webhooks/0/matchConditions/-",
    "value": {
      "name": "production-only",
      "expression": "object.metadata.namespace == \"anihpj-production\""
    }
  }
]'
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-location-network-injector patched
Example 5: Patch JobPost Webhook Reinvocation Policy

Change the reinvocation policy for the JobPost security enforcement webhook.

# Patch JobPost webhook reinvocation policy
kubectl patch mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer --type merge -p '
{
  "webhooks": [{
    "name": "jobpost-security.anihpj.svc",
    "reinvocationPolicy": "IfNeeded"
  }]
}'
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer patched

delete - delete a MutatingWebhookConfiguration

Delete a MutatingWebhookConfiguration to unregister admission webhooks and stop intercepting API requests.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/mutatingwebhookconfigurations/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path, string, required): Name of the MutatingWebhookConfiguration to delete
  • body (DeleteOptions): Options for deletion (grace period, propagation policy)
  • dryRun (in query, string): Simulate deletion without removing (All, None)
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query, integer): Grace period before deletion in seconds
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query, boolean): Ignore store read errors
  • pretty (in query, string): If 'true', prettify the JSON output
  • propagationPolicy (in query, string): Deletion propagation policy (Orphan, Background, Foreground)
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK - Configuration successfully deleted
  • 202 (Status): Accepted - Deletion accepted, in progress
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete Skills Sidecar Injector

Remove the Skills model sidecar injection webhook from the cluster.

# Delete Skills sidecar injection webhook
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector" deleted
Example 2: Delete User Webhook with Wait

Delete the User resource enforcement webhook and wait for completion.

# Delete User webhook and wait
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-user-resource-enforcer --wait=true

# Verify deletion
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-user-resource-enforcer
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-user-resource-enforcer" deleted Error from server (NotFound): mutatingwebhookconfigurations.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-user-resource-enforcer" not found
Example 3: Delete Author Webhook with Dry Run

Test deletion of the Author metadata injection webhook without actually removing it.

# Delete Author webhook with dry-run
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-author-label-injector --dry-run=server
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-author-label-injector" deleted (server dry run)
Example 4: Force Delete Location Webhook

Force delete the Location network injection webhook immediately with grace period 0.

# Force delete Location webhook
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration anihpj-location-network-injector --grace-period=0 --force
warning: Immediate deletion does not wait for confirmation that the running resource has been terminated. The resource may continue to run on the cluster indefinitely. mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-location-network-injector" force deleted
Example 5: Delete JobPost Webhook from File

Delete the JobPost security enforcement webhook using its YAML definition file.

# Delete JobPost webhook from file
kubectl delete -f jobpost-security-enforcer.yaml

# Confirm deletion
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l model=JobPost
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer" deleted No resources found

deletecollection - delete collection of MutatingWebhookConfiguration

Delete multiple MutatingWebhookConfiguration objects matching label or field selectors in a single operation.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/mutatingwebhookconfigurations
Parameters
  • body (DeleteOptions): Options for deletion (grace period, propagation policy)
  • continue (in query, string): Token for retrieving next page of results
  • dryRun (in query, string): Simulate deletion without removing (All, None)
  • fieldSelector (in query, string): Filter by field (e.g., metadata.name=anihpj-skills)
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query, integer): Grace period before deletion in seconds
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query, boolean): Ignore store read errors
  • labelSelector (in query, string): Filter by labels (e.g., app=anihpj,model=Skills)
  • limit (in query, integer): Maximum number of results to return
  • pretty (in query, string): If 'true', prettify the JSON output
  • propagationPolicy (in query, string): Deletion propagation policy (Orphan, Background, Foreground)
  • resourceVersion (in query, string): Resource version for deletion consistency
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query, string): How to match resource version (NotOlderThan, Exact)
  • sendInitialEvents (in query, boolean): Send synthetic events for initial state
  • timeoutSeconds (in query, integer): Timeout for deletion operation
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK - Collection successfully deleted
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete All anihpj Webhooks

Remove all mutating webhook configurations for the anihpj Django application.

# Delete all anihpj webhook configurations
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-author-label-injector" deleted mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer" deleted mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-location-network-injector" deleted mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector" deleted mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-user-resource-enforcer" deleted
Example 2: Delete Injection Purpose Webhooks with Dry Run

Test deletion of all sidecar and metadata injection webhooks without removing them.

# Delete injection webhooks with dry-run
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj,purpose=~injection --dry-run=server

# Alternative using grep-style matching (client-side)
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l app=anihpj -o name | \
  grep -E '(sidecar|metadata|label)-inject' | \
  xargs kubectl delete --dry-run=server
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-author-label-injector" deleted (server dry run) mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector" deleted (server dry run)
Example 3: Delete Webhooks by Model (Skills and User)

Remove webhook configurations for specific models during service decommissioning.

# Delete Skills and User model webhooks
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l 'app=anihpj,model in (Skills,User)'
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector" deleted mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-user-resource-enforcer" deleted
Example 4: Delete Webhooks Matching Name Pattern

Remove all webhook configurations matching a specific naming pattern.

# Delete webhooks with 'enforcer' in the name
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations -o name | grep enforcer | xargs kubectl delete
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-security-enforcer" deleted mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-user-resource-enforcer" deleted
Example 5: Delete Non-Security Webhooks

Remove all webhook configurations except those with security-related purposes.

# Delete webhooks that are not security-related (using inverse label selector)
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfigurations -l 'app=anihpj,purpose!=security-enforcement,purpose!=network-security'
mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-author-label-injector" deleted mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-sidecar-injector" deleted mutatingwebhookconfiguration.admissionregistration.k8s.io "anihpj-user-resource-enforcer" deleted

Role Overview

RoleList - Collection of Roles
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: Role v1 API | API Version: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1

Role is a namespaced, logical grouping of PolicyRules that can be referenced as a unit by a RoleBinding.

Full Description

Role provides namespace-scoped access control for your anihpj Django application, enabling fine-grained permissions within specific namespaces. Each Role in your jobpost application defines what actions can be performed on cluster resources, making it essential for securing your Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost services.

By creating dedicated Roles, you can follow the principle of least privilege, ensuring that each service only has access to the resources it needs. For example, your Skills service might need a Role that allows reading ConfigMaps and creating Pods, while your JobPost service requires a Role with broader permissions for managing Deployments and Services within its namespace.

Syntax

# Create a Role from YAML file
kubectl create -f role.yaml
# Create a Role imperatively
kubectl create role {name} --verb={verb} --resource={resource} --namespace={namespace}
# List all Roles in a namespace
kubectl get roles -n {namespace}
# List all Roles (short form)
kubectl get roles
# Get detailed information about a specific Role
kubectl describe role {name} -n {namespace}
# Get Role in YAML format
kubectl get role {name} -n {namespace} -o yaml
# Delete a specific Role
kubectl delete role {name} -n {namespace}

YAML Structure

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: anihpj-production
  name: anihpj-jobpost-manager
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost
    tier: backend
spec:
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "configmaps", "secrets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: ["apps"]
  resources: ["deployments", "replicasets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["services"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]

Parameters Description

  • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object's metadata
  • rules ([]PolicyRule): Rules holds all the PolicyRules for this Role (atomic: will be replaced during a merge)
  • rules.apiGroups ([]string): APIGroups containing the resources ("" for core, "*" for all)
  • rules.resources ([]string): List of resources this rule applies to ("*" for all)
  • rules.verbs ([]string), required: List of verbs that apply to ALL ResourceKinds ("*" for all)
  • rules.resourceNames ([]string): Optional white list of names (empty means everything allowed)
  • rules.nonResourceURLs ([]string): Set of partial URLs for non-resource access

Return Value

  • 200 (Role): OK - Request successful, Role returned
  • 201 (Role): Created - Role successfully created
  • 202 (Role): Accepted - Request accepted for processing
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Service Manager Role

This example demonstrates creating a dedicated Role for the Skills model service in the jobpost app.

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: anihpj-production
  name: anihpj-skills-manager
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: skills
    model: Skills
spec:
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "configmaps", "secrets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: ["apps"]
  resources: ["deployments"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "update", "patch"]
# Create Role for Skills service
kubectl create -f skills-manager-role.yaml
# Verify the Role was created
kubectl get role anihpj-skills-manager -n anihpj-production
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-manager created NAME CREATED AT anihpj-skills-manager 2024-11-10T10:30:45Z
Example 2: User Service Reader Role

This example shows creating a read-only Role for User model authentication service.

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: anihpj-shared
  name: anihpj-user-reader
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: user
    model: User
    permission: readonly
spec:
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["configmaps", "secrets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["services", "endpoints"]
  verbs: ["get", "list"]
# Create Role for User service
kubectl create -f user-reader-role.yaml
# Get detailed information about the Role
kubectl describe role anihpj-user-reader -n anihpj-shared
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-user-reader created Name: anihpj-user-reader Labels: app=anihpj model=User permission=readonly service=user Annotations: PolicyRule: Resources Non-Resource URLs Resource Names Verbs --------- ----------------- -------------- ----- configmaps [] [] [get list watch] secrets [] [] [get list watch] endpoints [] [] [get list] services [] [] [get list]
Example 3: Author Service Specific Resource Role

This example demonstrates creating a Role for the Author model with specific resource access.

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: anihpj-content
  name: anihpj-author-specific
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: author
    model: Author
    scope: specific
spec:
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["configmaps"]
  resourceNames: ["anihpj-author-config", "anihpj-content-config"]
  verbs: ["get", "update"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["secrets"]
  resourceNames: ["anihpj-author-db-secret"]
  verbs: ["get"]
# Create specific Role for Author service
kubectl create -f author-specific-role.yaml
# Get Role details in YAML format
kubectl get role anihpj-author-specific -n anihpj-content -o yaml
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-author-specific created apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: Role metadata: creationTimestamp: "2024-11-10T10:32:15Z" labels: app: anihpj model: Author scope: specific service: author name: anihpj-author-specific namespace: anihpj-content resourceVersion: "12845" uid: f3e4d2c1-8b9a-4c5e-9f2g-1h3i4j5k6l7m rules: - apiGroups: - "" resourceNames: - anihpj-author-config - anihpj-content-config resources: - configmaps verbs: - get - update - apiGroups: - "" resourceNames: - anihpj-author-db-secret resources: - secrets verbs: - get
Example 4: Location Service Development Role

This example shows creating a comprehensive Role for the Location model in development environment.

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: anihpj-development
  name: anihpj-location-dev
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: location
    model: Location
    environment: development
spec:
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["*"]
  verbs: ["*"]
- apiGroups: ["apps"]
  resources: ["deployments", "replicasets", "daemonsets"]
  verbs: ["*"]
- apiGroups: ["networking.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["ingresses"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch"]
# Create development Role for Location service
kubectl create -f location-dev-role.yaml
# List all Roles in development namespace
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-development
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-location-dev created NAME CREATED AT anihpj-location-dev 2024-11-10T10:33:20Z default-role 2024-11-09T08:15:30Z monitoring-role 2024-11-09T14:22:10Z
Example 5: JobPost Service Multi-API Group Role

This example demonstrates creating a Role for the JobPost model spanning multiple API groups.

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: anihpj-jobs
  name: anihpj-jobpost-multi-api
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost
    model: JobPost
    scope: multi-api
spec:
rules:
- apiGroups: ["", "apps", "extensions"]
  resources: ["pods", "deployments", "services", "configmaps"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: ["batch"]
  resources: ["jobs", "cronjobs"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: ["autoscaling"]
  resources: ["horizontalpodautoscalers"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "create", "update"]
# Create multi-API Role for JobPost service
kubectl create -f jobpost-multi-api-role.yaml
# Get detailed information about the Role
kubectl describe role anihpj-jobpost-multi-api -n anihpj-jobs
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-multi-api created Name: anihpj-jobpost-multi-api Labels: app=anihpj model=JobPost scope=multi-api service=jobpost Annotations: PolicyRule: Resources Non-Resource URLs Resource Names Verbs --------- ----------------- -------------- ----- configmaps [] [] [get list watch] deployments.apps [] [] [get list watch] deployments.extensions [] [] [get list watch] pods [] [] [get list watch] services [] [] [get list watch] cronjobs.batch [] [] [get list watch create update patch delete] jobs.batch [] [] [get list watch create update patch delete] horizontalpodautoscalers.autoscaling [] [] [get list create update]

RoleList

RoleList is a collection of Roles.

YAML Structure

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleList
metadata:
  resourceVersion: "12345"
items:
- apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
  kind: Role
  metadata:
    name: anihpj-skills-manager
    namespace: anihpj-production
  rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["pods", "configmaps"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
  kind: Role
  metadata:
    name: anihpj-user-reader
    namespace: anihpj-production
  rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["secrets"]
    verbs: ["get"]

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version, always "rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1" for RoleList
  • kind: string - Resource type, always "RoleList"
  • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata including resourceVersion
  • items ([]Role), required: List of Role objects

Return Value

  • 200 (RoleList): OK - Request successful, RoleList returned
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: List Skills Roles

This example demonstrates listing all Roles for the Skills model service.

# List all Skills Roles
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-production -l service=skills
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-skills-manager 2024-11-10T10:30:45Z anihpj-skills-reader 2024-11-09T14:20:30Z
Example 2: List User Roles with Details

This example shows listing User model Roles with detailed output.

# List User Roles with wide output
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-shared -l model=User -o wide
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-user-reader 2024-11-10T09:15:20Z anihpj-user-admin 2024-11-08T11:45:10Z
Example 3: List Author Roles in YAML

This example illustrates listing Author model Roles in YAML format.

# Get Author RoleList as YAML
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-content -l model=Author -o yaml
apiVersion: v1 items: - apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: Role metadata: name: anihpj-author-specific namespace: anihpj-content rules: - apiGroups: [""] resources: ["configmaps"] verbs: ["get", "update"] kind: List metadata: resourceVersion: ""
Example 4: List Location Roles by Label

This example demonstrates filtering Location model Roles using label selectors.

# List Location Roles by environment label
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-development -l model=Location,environment=development
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-location-dev 2024-11-10T10:33:20Z
Example 5: List All JobPost Roles Across Namespaces

This example shows listing all JobPost model Roles across all namespaces.

# List all JobPost Roles in all namespaces
kubectl get roles --all-namespaces -l model=JobPost
NAMESPACE NAME CREATED AT anihpj-jobs anihpj-jobpost-multi-api 2024-11-10T10:35:15Z anihpj-prod anihpj-jobpost-manager 2024-11-09T16:20:40Z

Role Operations

Operations Overview

get - Read the specified Role
list - List Roles in namespace
list all - List Roles in all namespaces
create - Create a Role
update - Replace the specified Role
patch - Partially update the specified Role
delete - Delete a Role
deletecollection - Delete collection of Roles

Operations allow you to interact with Role resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to manage authorization policies for your anihpj Django application, ensuring proper access control to cluster resources within namespaces.

get - read the specified Role

Read details of a specific Role by name within a namespace.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/roles/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the Role
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace of the Role
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (Role): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Skills Role Details

This example demonstrates retrieving details of the Skills model Role.

# Get Skills Role
kubectl get role anihpj-skills-manager -n anihpj-production -o yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: Role metadata: name: anihpj-skills-manager namespace: anihpj-production labels: app: anihpj model: Skills rules: - apiGroups: [""] resources: ["pods", "configmaps", "secrets"] verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"]
Example 2: Get User Role with Rules

This example shows retrieving User Role with detailed rules information.

# Get User Role details
kubectl describe role anihpj-user-reader -n anihpj-shared
Name: anihpj-user-reader Labels: app=anihpj model=User permission=readonly Annotations: PolicyRule: Resources Non-Resource URLs Resource Names Verbs --------- ----------------- -------------- ----- configmaps [] [] [get list watch] secrets [] [] [get list watch]
Example 3: Get Author Role JSON

This example illustrates retrieving Author Role in JSON format.

# Get Author Role as JSON
kubectl get role anihpj-author-specific -n anihpj-content -o json
{ "apiVersion": "rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1", "kind": "Role", "metadata": { "name": "anihpj-author-specific", "namespace": "anihpj-content", "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "model": "Author" } }, "rules": [ { "apiGroups": [""], "resources": ["configmaps"], "resourceNames": ["anihpj-author-config"], "verbs": ["get", "update"] } ] }
Example 4: Get Location Role Custom Output

This example demonstrates retrieving Location Role with custom columns.

# Get Location Role with custom output
kubectl get role anihpj-location-dev -n anihpj-development -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,CREATED:.metadata.creationTimestamp
NAME NAMESPACE CREATED anihpj-location-dev anihpj-development 2024-11-10T10:33:20Z
Example 5: Get JobPost Role JSONPath

This example shows retrieving specific JobPost Role fields using JSONPath.

# Get JobPost Role rules using JSONPath
kubectl get role anihpj-jobpost-multi-api -n anihpj-jobs -o jsonpath='{.rules[*].verbs}'
[get list watch] [get list watch create update patch delete] [get list create update]

list - list or watch objects of kind Role

List Roles in a specific namespace with optional filtering and pagination.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/roles
Parameters
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace to list Roles from
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - allow watch bookmarks
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter results
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter results
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - how to match resource version
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (RoleList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Namespace List Examples
Example 1: List Skills Roles in Production

This example demonstrates listing Skills model Roles in production namespace.

# List Skills Roles in production namespace
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-production -l model=Skills
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-skills-manager 2024-11-10T10:30:45Z anihpj-skills-reader 2024-11-09T14:20:30Z
Example 2: List User Roles with Label Filter

This example shows listing User Roles filtered by permission label.

# List readonly User Roles
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-shared -l model=User,permission=readonly
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-user-reader 2024-11-10T09:15:20Z
Example 3: List Author Roles with Wide Output

This example illustrates listing Author Roles with extended information.

# List Author Roles with wide output
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-content -l service=author -o wide
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-author-specific 2024-11-10T10:32:15Z anihpj-author-manager 2024-11-09T15:10:45Z
Example 4: List Location Development Roles

This example demonstrates listing Location Roles in development environment.

# List Location development Roles
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-development -l model=Location,environment=development
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-location-dev 2024-11-10T10:33:20Z
Example 5: List JobPost Roles with Limit

This example shows listing JobPost Roles with pagination limit.

# List JobPost Roles with limit
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-jobs -l model=JobPost --limit=5
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-jobpost-multi-api 2024-11-10T10:35:15Z anihpj-jobpost-manager 2024-11-09T16:20:40Z anihpj-jobpost-reader 2024-11-09T12:30:25Z

list - list or watch objects of kind Role (All Namespaces)

List Roles across all namespaces in the cluster.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/roles
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - allow watch bookmarks
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter results
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter results
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - how to match resource version
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (RoleList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Multi-Namespace Examples
Example 1: List All Skills Roles

This example demonstrates listing all Skills model Roles across all namespaces.

# List all Skills Roles in all namespaces
kubectl get roles --all-namespaces -l service=skills
NAMESPACE NAME CREATED AT anihpj-production anihpj-skills-manager 2024-11-10T10:30:45Z anihpj-development anihpj-skills-dev 2024-11-09T14:20:30Z
Example 2: List All User Roles

This example shows listing all User model Roles across environments.

# List all User Roles
kubectl get roles -A -l model=User
NAMESPACE NAME CREATED AT anihpj-shared anihpj-user-reader 2024-11-10T09:15:20Z anihpj-prod anihpj-user-admin 2024-11-08T11:45:10Z
Example 3: List All Author Roles with App Label

This example illustrates listing all Author Roles filtered by app label.

# List all Author Roles for anihpj app
kubectl get roles --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj,model=Author
NAMESPACE NAME CREATED AT anihpj-content anihpj-author-specific 2024-11-10T10:32:15Z anihpj-content anihpj-author-manager 2024-11-09T15:10:45Z
Example 4: List All Location Roles by Environment

This example demonstrates listing Location Roles across all environments.

# List all Location Roles
kubectl get roles -A -l model=Location
NAMESPACE NAME CREATED AT anihpj-development anihpj-location-dev 2024-11-10T10:33:20Z anihpj-production anihpj-location-prod 2024-11-09T18:25:15Z
Example 5: List All JobPost Roles with Output

This example shows listing all JobPost Roles with custom output format.

# List all JobPost Roles with custom columns
kubectl get roles --all-namespaces -l service=jobpost -o custom-columns=NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp
NAMESPACE NAME AGE anihpj-jobs anihpj-jobpost-multi-api 2024-11-10T10:35:15Z anihpj-prod anihpj-jobpost-manager 2024-11-09T16:20:40Z

create - create a Role

Create a new Role in a namespace to define permissions.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/roles
Parameters
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace to create the Role in
  • body: Role, required - Role object to create
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager name
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - field validation policy
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (Role): OK
  • 201 (Role): Created
  • 202 (Role): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Create Role Examples
Example 1: Create Skills Manager Role

This example demonstrates creating a manager Role for Skills model service.

# Create Skills manager Role
kubectl create role anihpj-skills-manager --verb=get,list,watch,create,update --resource=pods,configmaps -n anihpj-production
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-manager created
Example 2: Create User Reader Role from YAML

This example shows creating a readonly Role for User authentication service.

# Create User reader Role from file
kubectl create -f user-reader-role.yaml
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-user-reader created
Example 3: Create Author Specific Role with Dry Run

This example illustrates creating an Author Role with dry run validation.

# Create Author Role with dry run
kubectl create -f author-specific-role.yaml --dry-run=client -o yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: Role metadata: name: anihpj-author-specific namespace: anihpj-content rules: - apiGroups: [""] resources: ["configmaps"] resourceNames: ["anihpj-author-config"] verbs: ["get", "update"]
Example 4: Create Location Development Role

This example demonstrates creating a comprehensive development Role for Location service.

# Create Location development Role
kubectl create role anihpj-location-dev --verb=* --resource=* -n anihpj-development
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-location-dev created
Example 5: Create JobPost Multi-API Role

This example shows creating a multi-API group Role for JobPost operations.

# Create JobPost multi-API Role from file
kubectl apply -f jobpost-multi-api-role.yaml
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-multi-api created

update - replace the specified Role

Replace an existing Role with a new definition.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/roles/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the Role
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace of the Role
  • body: Role, required - updated Role object
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager name
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - field validation policy
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (Role): OK
  • 201 (Role): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Update Role Examples
Example 1: Update Skills Role Permissions

This example demonstrates updating Skills Role to add delete permission.

# Update Skills Role from file
kubectl apply -f skills-manager-role-updated.yaml
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-manager configured
Example 2: Update User Role Resources

This example shows updating User Role to include additional resources.

# Replace User Role
kubectl replace -f user-reader-role-v2.yaml
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-user-reader replaced
Example 3: Update Author Role Verbs

This example illustrates updating Author Role with new verbs.

# Update Author Role
kubectl apply -f author-specific-role-updated.yaml
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-author-specific configured
Example 4: Update Location Role API Groups

This example demonstrates updating Location Role to include more API groups.

# Replace Location Role with new definition
kubectl replace -f location-dev-role-v2.yaml
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-location-dev replaced
Example 5: Update JobPost Role Rules

This example shows updating JobPost Role with modified rules.

# Update JobPost Role
kubectl apply -f jobpost-multi-api-role-v2.yaml
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-multi-api configured

patch - partially update the specified Role

Partially update a Role without replacing the entire object.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/roles/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the Role
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace of the Role
  • body: Patch, required - patch data
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager name
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - field validation policy
  • force (in query): boolean - force update
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (Role): OK
  • 201 (Role): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Patch Role Examples
Example 1: Patch Skills Role Labels

This example demonstrates patching Skills Role to add new labels.

# Patch Skills Role labels
kubectl patch role anihpj-skills-manager -n anihpj-production --type merge -p '{"metadata":{"labels":{"version":"v2"}}}'
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-manager patched
Example 2: Patch User Role Add Verb

This example shows patching User Role to add a new verb to existing rules.

# Patch User Role to add delete verb
kubectl patch role anihpj-user-reader -n anihpj-shared --type json -p='[{"op":"add","path":"/rules/0/verbs/-","value":"delete"}]'
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-user-reader patched
Example 3: Patch Author Role Annotations

This example illustrates patching Author Role to update annotations.

# Patch Author Role annotations
kubectl patch role anihpj-author-specific -n anihpj-content -p '{"metadata":{"annotations":{"description":"Updated author permissions"}}}'
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-author-specific patched
Example 4: Patch Location Role Add Resource

This example demonstrates patching Location Role to include additional resources.

# Patch Location Role to add resource
kubectl patch role anihpj-location-dev -n anihpj-development --type json -p='[{"op":"add","path":"/rules/0/resources/-","value":"services"}]'
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-location-dev patched
Example 5: Patch JobPost Role Add Rule

This example shows patching JobPost Role to add a completely new rule.

# Patch JobPost Role to add new rule
kubectl patch role anihpj-jobpost-multi-api -n anihpj-jobs --type json -p='[{"op":"add","path":"/rules/-","value":{"apiGroups":["networking.k8s.io"],"resources":["ingresses"],"verbs":["get","list"]}}]'
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-multi-api patched

delete - delete a Role

Delete a specific Role from a namespace.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/roles/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the Role
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace of the Role
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period in seconds
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 202 (Status): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Delete Role Examples
Example 1: Delete Skills Development Role

This example demonstrates deleting a temporary Skills development Role.

# Delete Skills development Role
kubectl delete role anihpj-skills-dev -n anihpj-development
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-dev" deleted
Example 2: Delete User Test Role with Dry Run

This example shows deleting User test Role with dry run validation.

# Delete User test Role with dry run
kubectl delete role anihpj-user-test -n anihpj-shared --dry-run=client
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-user-test" deleted (dry run)
Example 3: Delete Author Temporary Role

This example illustrates deleting a temporary Author Role.

# Delete Author temporary Role
kubectl delete role anihpj-author-temp -n anihpj-content
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-author-temp" deleted
Example 4: Delete Location Staging Role

This example demonstrates deleting Location staging environment Role.

# Delete Location staging Role
kubectl delete role anihpj-location-staging -n anihpj-staging
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-location-staging" deleted
Example 5: Delete JobPost Old Version Role

This example shows deleting an outdated JobPost Role version.

# Delete JobPost old version Role
kubectl delete role anihpj-jobpost-v1 -n anihpj-jobs
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-v1" deleted

deletecollection - delete collection of Role

Delete multiple Roles matching label selector criteria.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/roles
Parameters
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace to delete Roles from
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • continue (in query): string - continue token
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period in seconds
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector
  • limit (in query): integer - limit
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Collection Delete Examples
Example 1: Delete All Skills Test Roles

This example demonstrates deleting all Skills test Roles using label selector.

# Delete all Skills test Roles
kubectl delete roles -n anihpj-development -l model=Skills,environment=test
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-test-1" deleted role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-test-2" deleted
Example 2: Delete All User Development Roles

This example shows deleting all User development Roles with dry run.

# Delete User development Roles with dry run
kubectl delete roles -n anihpj-shared -l model=User,environment=dev --dry-run=client
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-user-dev-1" deleted (dry run) role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-user-dev-2" deleted (dry run)
Example 3: Delete All Author Temporary Roles

This example illustrates deleting temporary Author Roles based on tier label.

# Delete temporary Author Roles
kubectl delete roles -n anihpj-content -l model=Author,tier=temporary
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-author-temp-reader" deleted role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-author-temp-writer" deleted
Example 4: Delete All Location Old Version Roles

This example demonstrates deleting old version Location Roles during upgrade.

# Delete old version Location Roles
kubectl delete roles -n anihpj-development -l model=Location,version=v1.0
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-location-v1-manager" deleted role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-location-v1-reader" deleted
Example 5: Delete All JobPost Non-Production Roles

This example shows deleting all non-production JobPost Roles.

# List what would be deleted
kubectl get roles -n anihpj-jobs -l model=JobPost,environment!=production
# Delete non-production JobPost Roles
kubectl delete roles -n anihpj-jobs -l model=JobPost,environment!=production
NAME AGE anihpj-jobpost-dev 2d anihpj-jobpost-staging 1d role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-dev" deleted role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-staging" deleted
